========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/2.0 Reference Tables/2.4 Conditions.md
========================================================================

#### What conditions are 
Conditions are status effects that change what a creature can do or impose some effect on the affected person for the duration. 
#### Stacking and contradictions
- If a creature has multiple status effects at once, resolve each independently.
- If two effects contradict one another (e.g., Hasted and Slowed), they cancel each other.
- You may have multiple levels or stacks of a condition if the condition specifies that it is possible. 
	- A condition that cannot be stacked will simply gain more time of it being active if reapplied
	- A condition that can be stacked will increase how long it is active as well as other potential effects
	- If a condition is stacked from a separate source with a separate DC or end condition the higher DC or more penalized end condition takes priority and effects the rest of the condition. For example, if you have 3 stacks of regular bleeding but then a spell is cast on you to give you bleeding with a DC of 15 for 4 turns you will now have 7 stacks of bleeding and the higher DC applies to all of it. 

The DC to remove a condition may be different if specified by the source of the condition, such as a powerful spell but the specified DC is the default when none is provided. If a condition specifies a time (Lasts 3 turns) then it can still be removed with remove / end unless otherwise stated in the skill. 

If a damage type causes a condition which causes a the same damage type it will not cause the condition again. Conditions cannot activate/be caused by themselves. 
### Environmental / Positional Conditions
All conditions which deal damage deal it straight to health bypassing vitality. 

| Condition     | Effect                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Removal / End                                                                        |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| On Fire       | Take 1d6 Fire damage at the start of your turn. After condition ends gain Burned condition                                                                                                                                                              | Take water damage or wait 3 turns after no longer being in contact with fire source  |
| Frozen        | Movement speed is halved and BAp are halved. Can be stacked, each stack does 1d4 Ice/Cold damage per turn. If stacks are greater than 3 then when condition ends gain Frostbitten for 1 turn                                                            | Take fire damage or wait 2 turns                                                     |
| Soaked        | Take double damage from Lightning damage and half damage from Fire damage                                                                                                                                                                               | Take fire damage or wait 10 turns after no longer being in contact with water source |
| Storm Shocked | As long as you have soaked condition take 1d4 Lightning damage per turn                                                                                                                                                                                 | Ends when Soaked condition does                                                      |
| Hurting       | Take 1 Force damage per turn. Can be stacked, each stack increases damage per turn by 1                                                                                                                                                                 | Wait 2 turns                                                                         |
| Entangled     | Movement is 0                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | DC 10 Athletics Check                                                                |
| Suffocating   | Take 1d6 Vacuum damage at the start of your turn and have disadvantage on attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks, additionally gain the mute condition                                                                                           | DC 10 Constitution Throw                                                             |
| Prone         | Enemies have advantage to hit you and you have disadvantage on all rolls. You automatically fail all saving throws. Moving at all ends condition but costs half of your total movement                                                                  | Spend half of normal total movement to stand up                                      |
| Grappled      | Movement is 0, enemies have advantage to hit you and you cannot make melee or ranged attacks until grapple ends. While grappled the creature who is grappling you always deals damage to HP.                                                            | Win combative athletics check against grappler                                       |
| Lethargic     | Movement is 0, cannot take Actions or Reactions                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Wait 1 turn                                                                          |
| Unconscious   | Cannot do anything, all damage taken goes straight to HP. If you were not forced into this state you may end it at any time by choice. Each time you take damage after being forced Unconscious or on the start of your turn you may make the end save. | DC 15 Wis Save                                                                       |
| Surprised     | Movement is 0, cannot take Actions, Bonus Actions or Reactions                                                                                                                                                                                          | Wait 1 turn                                                                          |
| Threatened    | Happened whenever you are within 5 ft of an enemy. While you have Threatened your ranged attacks and ranged spell attacks have disadvantage to hit                                                                                                      | Don't be within 5 ft of an enemy                                                     |
| Invisible     | You cannot be directly targeted, creatures cannot see you. Creatures do not know where you are, only your last known position unless they have a specific skill that allows them to sense you. You have advantage on attack rolls.                      | Make an attack against any creature                                                  |
| Intangible    | You are immune to Physical damage and cannot interact with the physical world. You cannot make melee or ranged attacks.                                                                                                                                 | End the condition when you please                                                    |
| Grounded      | You cannot fly, teleport, or be moved by any magical effect, and your jump height becomes 0.                                                                                                                                                            | Wait 1 hour or leave thing that is grounding you                                     |
### Physical Conditions
All conditions which deal damage deal it straight to health bypassing vitality. 

| Condition   | Effect                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Removal / End                                                  |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Bleeding    | -1d4 health at the start of your turn, can be stacked. Each stack adds an additional -1d4 health per turn.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | DC 10 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Pierced     | -3 AC for duration                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | DC 10 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Burned      | Take 1d4 fire damage at the start of your turn                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | DC 10 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Frostbitten | Take 1d4 Ice/Cold damage at the start of your turn, movement is halved, can be stacked, each stack increases damage by +1d4 and DC for removal by 1.                                                                                                                                                                                                         | DC 10 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Shocked     | Take 1d4 lightning damage at the start of your turn, disadvantage on attack rolls                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | DC 10 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Poisoned    | Take 1d6 poison damage at the start of your turn, disadvantage on skill checks. This condition can be stacked dealing an additional 1d4 poison damage per stack. The DC for removal raises by 2 for each stack.                                                                                                                                              | DC 10 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Diseased    | Take 1d6 blight damage at the start of your turn, disadvantage on saving throws                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | DC 10 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Hellfire    | Take 1d8 infernal damage at the start of your turn                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | DC 15 medicine check                                           |
| Purged      | All positive buffs are removed and cannot be regained during the duration. Cannot regain Vitality or Health. Gain weakness to Hallowed damage. If undead you now take 4x damage from hallowed instead of 3x.                                                                                                                                                 | DC 15 Wis save                                                 |
| Corrupt     | Take 1d4 corruption damage at the start of your turn and gain weakness to all damage types                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | DC 20 medicine check                                           |
| Necrosis    | Take 1d8 necrotic damage at the start of your turn, disadvantage on saving throws                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | DC 13 medicine check or any healing spell/skill                |
| Blinded     | Triple disadvantage on attack rolls and automatically fail Search and Spot checks                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Wait 3 turns                                                   |
| Deafened    | Disadvantage on attack rolls, automatically fail any checks reliant on sound (GM's digression)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Wait 3 turns                                                   |
| Paralyzed   | Movement is 0 and cannot take Actions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Wait 3 turns                                                   |
| Petrified   | Cannot do anything (turn is skipped)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Wait 5 turns                                                   |
| Stunned     | Movement is 0 and cannot take Actions, Bonus Actions or Reactions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Wait 1 turn                                                    |
| Gelled      | Movement is halved and Ap, BAp, and Rp are halved (minimum of 1, rounded down). You take double damage from Fire. Gelled can be stacked, each stack increases how much damage must be taken to remove the condition. On 1 stack any damage will remove it but for each additional stack you must take 10 more fire or water damage.                          | Take Fire or water damage                                      |
| Radiation   | Take 1d6 Atomic damage at the start of your turn. Stacks. Each additional stack adds +1d6 Atomic damage per turn. While Radiated, you have disadvantage on saving throws and you cannot regain Vitality by any means. You cannot remove Radiated by waiting. Removing radiation from a medicine check can only remove 1 stack at a time per check.           | DC 25 Medicine Check. The removal DC increases by +5 per stack |
| Sickened    | Disadvantage on skill checks, you take 1 poison damage every hour.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | DC 15 medicine check or wait 24 hours                          |
| Withered    | Take 1d10+2 Blight damage at the start of each turn and have disadvantage on STR and CON saves and checks.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | DC 20 medicine check                                           |
| Plagued     | Disadvantage on skill checks, saving throws, and attack rolls, you take 1 miasma damage each turn. All damage you deal is halved, your speed is halved and your Vitality Max is halved during the duration of the effect. Can be stacked, each stack deals +5 damage per turn. Having greater than 3 stacks will deal 4 damage per turn to vitality as well. | DC 50 medicine check (or Nat 20)                               |
### Mental Conditions
All conditions which deal damage deal it straight to health bypassing vitality. 

| Condition     | Effect                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Removal / End                        |
| ------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |

| Frightened    | Must use movement to move away from caster, on each turn make a Wisdom Save (DC: 10) on fail become Paralyzed for the remainder of this turn.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | DC 10 Wisdom Save                    |

| Demoralized   | Disadvantage on all rolls (all dice, even damage dice).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | DC 10 Wisdom Save                    |

| Hypnotized    | Must obey one one word command from caster, the command may not be directly harmful to you.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | DC 10 Wisdom Save                    |

After two days they gain Despair and lose half of their Vitality Max until they take the substance again. The condition resets each time the substance is taken again. The conditions granted from this condition will effect the creature even if they are immune to them. If the creature does not partake in the substance after one week the DC for removal will be reduced by 5. Each subsequent week will decrease the DC by 5 again. | DC 30 Wis Save or Remove Curse spell |

All conditions which deal damage deal it straight to health bypassing vitality.

| Platinum | 10                               | 1000 Dollars  |
| Nertie   | ---                              | 10000 Dollars |
## Weapons
Your skill with a weapon is split into two layers: **proficiency** (by weapon type) and **expertise** (by specific weapon or by weapon type). They stack as follows:

- **Damage rolls** always add your Strength or Dexterity modifier (depending on the attack), regardless of proficiency.
- **Proficiency (by weapon type):** You add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to your attack roll, unless the weapon type specifies which you must use you may use either. Without proficiency, you do not add this modifier, you simply roll a flat d20.
	- **Example**: Proficiency with *Light* weapons lets you add your stat to attack rolls with Daggers, Shortswords, Scimitars, etc.
	- You are always considered proficient with unarmed strikes (fists) and any natural or racial attacks you possess (claws, bites, etc.).
	- If a weapon has multiple types listed you only have to have proficiency with one of its types to gain proficiency with it. 
- **Expertise (by specific weapon):** You gain Advantage on attack rolls with that **one weapon**. You must be proficient with its weapon type first.
	- Example: Expertise with *Longsword* gives Advantage on all Longsword attack rolls, but does not affect other swords.  You may only gain this expertise after being proficient in Versatile weapons.
	- If a weapon has multiple types listed you **must** have proficiency with **all** of its types to gain expertise with it. 
	- You cannot gain expertise in unarmed. 
- **Expertise (by weapon type):** You gain +2 to attack rolls with **every weapon of that type**. You must be proficient with that type first.
	- Example: Expertise with *Heavy* weapons gives +2 to attack rolls with Greatswords, Greataxes, Mauls, etc.
- These bonuses stack. If you have Expertise with a specific weapon and its type, you gain both Advantage and +2 on attack rolls with that weapon.

The following is a list of all weapon types:
- **Unarmed:** Your body, fists, kicks, claws, bites.
- **Light:** A small, quick weapon that can be used in your off-hand for dual wielding.
- **Finesse:** You may use your Dexterity modifier instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls.
- **Thrown:** Can be thrown as a ranged attack using the same stat modifier.
- **Versatile:** Can be wielded in two hands to deal the higher damage die shown in parentheses.
- **Two-Handed:** Requires both hands to wield.
- **Heavy:** Too large for Small creatures to use effectively; they have Disadvantage on attack rolls.
- **Reach:** Adds 5 ft (usually) to your melee reach when attacking with this weapon.
- **Martial:** A weapon with an unconventional design or technique, such as flails, morningstars, picks, and hooking swords.
- **Hooking:** You may hook shields, limbs, or objects. On a hit, you may attempt to pull or trip the target using a combative athletics check.
- **Ranged:** A ranged weapon that fires ammunition.
- **Loading:** Must be loaded before firing costing a Bonus Action (BAp).
- **Magical:** Enhances spellcasting. Adds its listed damage to spell attacks.
- **Restraining:** On a hit, the target must make a save or become Restrained.
- **Special:** Unique rules, see the weapon's description.

| **Name**        | **Base Damage**                 | **Base Range** | **Type(s)**                 | **Description**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | **Price (Iron)**      |
| --------------- | ------------------------------- | -------------- | --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |

## Shields
Shields require a free hand to use, so they cannot be used with two-handed weapons.

Proficiency and expertise with shields work as follows:
- **Proficiency:** You gain the shield's full AC bonus. Without proficiency, you gain half of the AC from the shield (rounding up) and attacks made while wielding it have Disadvantage.
- **Expertise:** You gain +1 AC with any shield you wield. This stacks with the shield's base AC and any material bonuses.

Shields are mostly made of wood but can be lined with other materials; the AC bonus from the material modifier is halved. Each shield type offers different coverage and handling, and some grant unique mechanical benefits.

| **Name**        | **Base AC** | **Description**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Price (Wood)** |
| --------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
| Buckler         | 1           | A tiny round shield (6–12 in) strapped to the forearm, leaving the hand free to climb, grapple, or interact. Favoured by duelists for active parrying. Your hand remains free while wielding this shield.                                                                                       | 1 Gold           |
| Small Shield    | 1           | A light shield, typically round or oval (12–18 in), strapped to the arm. Provides basic deflection with minimal weight. A simple, reliable choice for light skirmishers.                                                                                                                        | 2 Gold           |
| Medium Shield   | 2           | The standard infantry shield (18–24 in), offering solid torso coverage. Used by soldiers and adventurers who expect regular melee. The most versatile and commonly carried shield.                                                                                                              | 4 Gold           |
| Large Shield    | 3           | A substantial shield (30–36 in) covering most of the body. Its size makes it excellent for holding a line, but the extra weight reduces your speed by 5 ft.                                                                                                                                     | 6 Gold           |
| Tower Shield    | 4           | A massive full-body shield as tall as its wielder. You can deploy it as an action to plant it on the ground, granting 3/4 cover to creatures behind it. While deployed, you may use a one-handed ranged weapon from behind it. Reduces your speed by 10 ft.                                     | 10 Gold          |

You may make a shield bash as a bonus action, dealing 1d4 piercing damage using the spike (Damage scales with material, damage bonus halved).                                               | 3 Gold           |
## Armors
Armor proficiency is divided into three types: **Light**, **Medium**, and **Heavy**.

You gain proficiency with armor by type. Unless otherwise specified, you must gain these in order: **Light → Medium → Heavy**. You cannot gain Medium proficiency without Light, and you cannot gain Heavy proficiency without both Light and Medium.

If you are proficient with an armor, you ignore all effects listed in its Penalty category. If you are not proficient, all listed penalties apply as written.

You may gain Expertise with any armor type you are already proficient in. If you have Expertise with an armor, increase its AC bonus based on its type:

- **Light Armor:** +1 AC
- **Medium Armor:** +2 AC
- **Heavy Armor:** +4 AC

These bonuses are applied in addition to the armor’s listed AC value.

| **Name**        | **AC** | **Type** | **No Proficiency Penalty**  | **Max Dex Bonus** | **Max Dex Bonus (Expert)** | **Price (Iron)**             | Notes                                                                                                                                   |
| --------------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------- | ----------------- | -------------------------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Padded          | +1     | Light    | Stealth Disadvantage, -1 AC | +5                | +7                         | 1 Gold (Leather)             | Made from Leather, Wool, or Dragon Scales                                                                                               |
| Leather         | +1     | Light    | -1 AC                       | +5                | +7                         | 1 Gold (Leather)             | Made from Leather or Dragon Scales                                                                                                      |
| Studded Leather | +2     | Light    | -1 AC                       | +5                | +7                         | 2 Gold (Leather, Iron Studs) | Base made from Leather or any Chimera Hide. Studs can be metal granting half the metal's normal bonuses on top of base material bonuses |
| Hide            | +2     | Medium   | -1 AC                       | +4                | +6                         | 3 Gold                       | Made from any Chimera/Animal Hide only                                                                                                  |
| Chain Shirt     | +3     | Medium   | -1 AC                       | +4                | +6                         | 4 Gold                       | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
| Scale Mail      | +4     | Medium   | Stealth Disadvantage, -1 AC | +4                | +6                         | 1 Platinum                   | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
| Breastplate     | +4     | Medium   | -1 AC                       | +4                | +6                         | 1 Platinum                   | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
| Half-Plate      | +5     | Medium   | Stealth Disadvantage, -1 AC | +4                | +6                         | 3 Platinum                   | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
| Ring Mail       | +4     | Heavy    | Stealth Disadvantage, -1 AC | +3                | +5                         | 2 Platinum                   | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
| Chain Mail      | +6     | Heavy    | -5 ft Speed, -1 AC          | +3                | +5                         | 4 Platinum                   | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
| Splint          | +7     | Heavy    | -5 ft Speed, -1 AC          | +3                | +5                         | 5 Platinum                   | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
| Plate           | +8     | Heavy    | -10 ft Speed, -1 AC         | +3                | +5                         | 6 Platinum                   | Made from metal or Dragon Scales only                                                                                                   |
> Your AC is worked out like this:
>
> **10 + Armor + Dex Bonus + Shield + Other Bonuses**
>
> Here's what each part means:
> - **10**, your base. Everyone starts here.
> - **Armor**, the armor's base AC (from the table above), plus any AC bonus from its material (see [[2.6 Materials]]), plus your Expertise bonus if you have it (+1 Light, +2 Medium, +4 Heavy).
> - **Dex Bonus**, your Dexterity mod converted through the AC Bonus table in [[1.3 Player Stats]]. Your Dex mod doesn't always translate 1-to-1, and your armor type limits how much you can use (that's the Max Dex Mod column). Having Expertise in that armor raises the cap (the If Expert column), and the Dodge Master feat raises it even higher.
> - **Shield**, the shield's base AC plus half the AC bonus from its material (rounded down).
>
> Let's walk through some examples:
>
> **Starting out: Iron gear, no shield**
> - No armor? Just 10 + whatever your Dex gives you. Say you have 14 Dex (+2 mod → +2 AC). That's 10 + 2 = **12 AC**.
> - Now you find some Iron Plate (+8). No Expertise yet, 16 Dex (+3 mod → +3 AC). Heavy caps at +3, so you get all of it. 10 + 8 + 3 = **21 AC**.
> - You pick up Heavy Armor Expertise (+4). Same 16 Dex. 10 + 8 + 4 + 3 = **25 AC**.
> - You get to 20 Dex (+5 mod → +4 AC). Expertise cap is +5, so the full +4 applies. 10 + 8 + 4 + 4 = **26 AC**.
>
> **Late game: Nerite gear, with shield**
> - Upgrade that Plate to Nerite (adds +6). 10 + 8 + 6 + 4 + 4 = **32 AC**.
> - Take the Dodge Master feat, which adds +3 to your Dex AC bonus (capped at +7). Your Dex now contributes +7. 10 + 8 + 6 + 4 + 7 = **35 AC**.
> - Grab a Nerite Heater Shield (+3 base, +3 from half Nerite's AC bonus). 35 + 6 = **41 AC**.
> - Next grab the Shield Master feat to double the bonus from the shield (+6 base to +12). 35 + 12 = **47 AC**.
> - And that's before magic effects and other bonuses, also not even the best AC boosting materials, just high end ones!
>
> See [[1.3 Player Stats]] for the AC Bonus conversion table and the feats in [[3.3 Leveling Up]] for more.
## Mounts/Familiar Equipment
> See [[1.5 Mounts and Familiars]] for more.

| Name            | Price      | Description                         |
| --------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------- |
| Domestic Saddle | 1 Gold     | Can be used to ride Domestic Mounts |
| War Saddle      | 3 Gold     | Can be used to ride War Mounts      |
| Exotic Saddle   | 5 Gold     | Can be used to ride Exotic Mounts   |
| Flying Harness  | 5 Gold     | Can be used to ride flying Mounts   |
| Domestic Armor  | 5 Gold     | Mount gains +3 AC                   |
| War Armor       | 1 Platinum | Mount gains +3 AC                   |
| Exotic Armor    | 2 Platinum | Mount gains +3 AC                   |
## Tools/Equipment
Tools can be made from most materials listed in the Materials section. 

Proficiency and expertise with tools work as follows:
- **Proficiency:** You know how to use the tool for its intended purpose. When making a check the tool applies to, you add its material's Tool Modifier to the roll. Without proficiency, you do not add this modifier. The modifiers are listed in the material table in [[2.6 Materials]].
- **Expertise:** You have Advantage on checks made with that tool.

### Dragon Drops
> All dragon drops other than meat can be used to make armor, weapons, and tools, as such their modifiers are included here.

On a critical hit, the target and all creatures within 10 feet take an additional 2d6 radiant damage. Armor: Grants Greater Resistance to nova damage. Wearer emits bright light in a 20-foot radius and is immune to the blinded condition.                                                     | +12    | +10      | +12      | +14           | 15             | x50         | 3 Nerite    |
| Solar Dragon Scale      | Solar Dragons      | Golden orange scales that glow intensely. Looking directly at them leaves spots in your vision. The surface radiates steady heat.                     | Weapon: Changes damage type to solar. Weapon attacks deal an additional +1d10 solar damage. Damage is doubled against undead and fiends. Armor: Grants Greater Resistance to solar damage. Wearer is immune to exhaustion from heat exposure.                                                                                                                                              | +12    | +10      | +12      | +14           | 15             | x50         | 3 Nerite    |

Potions consumed by the wearer heal an additional 1d4 hit points.                                                                                           | +12    | +10      | +12      | +14           | 15             | x50         | 3 Nerite    |

========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/3.0 Character Management/3.1 Building Your Character.md
========================================================================

To start building your character you should write a backstory for your character then build a character sheet that fits your story. For the most part you will be starting at Level 1, that means you are a regular person, you have not accomplished many great things and if you have you should have some kind of in world explanation for how you did that while being so low level. Some campaigns may start at higher levels which allows your characters to have more backstory. 

Your character sheet will look something like this as a base, and we will work on filling out the blank parts together (for blank character sheets see [[2.7 Character Reference]]): 

| Character Information |     |                           |     | Stats                  | (Stat) | (Modifier) |
| --------------------- | --- | ------------------------- | --- | ---------------------- | ------ | ---------- |
| Name                  |     | Level                     | 1   | Strength               | 8      | -1         |
| Race                  |     | Inspiration               | 0   | Dexterity              | 8      | -1         |
| Background            |     | Armor Class               |     | Constitution           | 8      | -1         |
| Title(s)              |     | Initiative                |     | Wisdom                 | 8      | -1         |
| Sex                   |     | Speed                     |     | Intelligence           | 8      | -1         |
| Size                  |     | Karma                     |     | Charisma               | 8      | -1         |
| Height                |     | Proficiency Bonus         | +1  |                        |        |            |
| Weight                |     | Action Points (Ap)        | 1   | Stat Points (Stp)      | 24     |            |
| Build                 |     | Bonus Action Points (BAp) | 1   | Skill Points (Skp)     | 5      |            |
| Age                   |     | Reaction Points (Rp)      | 1   | Affinity Points (Affp) | 5      |            |

|                | Max | Current | Dice |     |         | Base Damage | Base Accuracy |
| -------------- | --- | ------- | ---- | --- | ------- | ----------- | ------------- |
| Vitality (Vit) |     |         | 1d8  |     | Melee   | 0           | 0             |
| Health (Hp)    |     |         | 1d6  |     | Ranged  | 0           | 0             |
| Mana (Mp)      |     |         | 1d6  |     | Magical | 0           | 0             |

| Affinities |     |           |     |            |     |          |     |           |     |            |
| ---------- | --- | --------- | --- | ---------- | --- | -------- | --- | --------- | --- | ---------- |
| Generic    |     | Lightning |     | Hallowed |     | Tremor   |     | Thunder   |     | Obsidian   |
| Fire       |     | Steam     |     | Starlight  |     | Deluge   |     | Mirage    |     | Quake      |
| Earth      |     | Magma     |     | Cursed     |     | Shatter  |     | Vacuum    |     | Corruption |
