|              | Situational Insight |     |             |         | \|  |              |                   |       |             |         |
## Filling Out Your Character Sheet
#### Step 1: Core Identity & Bio
Before crunching numbers, define the fundamental traits of your character.
- **Name & Title:** Choose your name and any starting titles, for more information about titles see [[3.2 Character Information]].
- **Race & Background:** Select a Race (e.g., Human, Elf) and a Background (e.g., Soldier, Scholar). For more information about backgrounds see [[3.2 Character Information]] and for more information about races see [[4.1 Races]] or look through the race lists and choose your favorite. 
- **Physical Characteristics:** Fill in Sex, Size, Height, Weight, age, and Build. Your height, weight, and age ranges will be different depending on which race you choose. 
- **Karma:** Determine your character's moral and ethical outlook. For more information on Alignment see [[3.2 Character Information]]. If you don't have a backstory yet you can worry about this later and just set it to 0 for now. 
#### Step 2: Determine Ability Stats
Your character has six core Stats. (Strength, Dexterity, etc) the standard baseline is 8 (+1).
- **Assign Scores:** First check your Race to see what stat bonuses you get (if any) and add them to your stats. You then are given 24 Stat Points (Stp) to spend at level 1 (located in the first table of your character sheet on the bottom right), you may put them into any of the main 6 stats as you choose, you may also remove from other stats but no starting stat can be lower than 4. For each point above 20 it will now cost 2 stat points to go up 1 point and above 30 it goes to 3 and so on. Please note you are not required to spend all of your points at this time, you may spend them at any time.
	- 0-20 costs 1 point
	- 21-30 costs 2 points
	- 31-40 costs 3 points
	- There is no finite cap to how high the numbers can go, just follow the pattern for higher numbers.

> **How stat buying actually works with an example**
>
> Let's say you want to build a strong fighter. You have 24 Stat Points and your race gives +2 Strength.
>
> **Step 1:** Start with 8 in all stats. That's the baseline.
> **Step 2:** Add your racial bonuses, for this example lets assume that gives you +2 to strength which puts it to 10. 
> **Step 3:** Now you get to add your points, you decide to raise strength. 10 to 20 costs 10 points (1 per point). Now lets say you want to go above 20, you can spend another 10 to get to 25 and now you have 5 points left. If you wanted to just spend everything in strength you'd end up with 27 strength and 1 point left over, you can hold on to it for now or dump it into another stat. 

- **Calculate Modifiers:** For every 2 points away from 10, the modifier changes by 1 (Or to calculate it take your stat subtract 10 and divide by 2).
    - _Example: 8 = -1, 10 = +0, 12 = +1, 14 = +2, etc._
#### Step 3: Calculate Combat Vitals
Use your Stats to fill in the top-middle section:
- **Level:** Start at Level 1 (unless otherwise instructed).
- **Armor Class (AC):** 10+Dexterity Modifier+Armor (You will not have armor usually at level 1 so ignore that for now)
- **Initiative:** Dexterity Modifier
- **Speed:** Determined by your Race
- **Inspiration:** Leave this blank; it is typically earned during gameplay.
- **Action Points (Ap):** Starts at 1
- **Bonus Action Points (BAp):** Starts at 1
- **Reaction Points (Rp):** Starts at 1
#### Step 4: Select Affinities
This system features a long list of 40+ Affinities, for now just worry about the first 7. Fire, earth, water, air, radiant, necrotic, psychic.
- **Assign Values:** Based on your Race mark your relationship with these elements.
	- **Negative Affinity:** -3
	- **Neutral Affinity:** 0
	- **Positive Affinity:** 3
- You are then given 5 Affinity Points (Affp) which you may assign to any of the affinities as you see fit (located next to your stat points on the bottom right of the first table in your character sheet). See [[2.1 Affinities]] to see what you can add points to. At the start you are unable to assign points to anything beyond the base 7. Please note you are not required to spend all of your points at this time, you may spend them at any time.
- Remember that you can never put points into your generic affinity modifier. 
#### Step 5: Skill Proficiencies & Misc
The Skills section is grouped by their governing Ability Stat.
- **Calculate Skill Mods:** Write the corresponding Ability Modifier in the Mod column for each skill.
- **Add Bonuses:** You are given 5 Skill Points (Skp) which you may use freely on any skills you would like. **The cost to go up in a skill point raises as the skill raises (see chart below).** Skills have a maximum modifier of 20. Please note you are not required to spend all of your points at this time, you may spend them at any time, life affinity and stat points they are located at the bottom right of the first table on your character sheet.
- **Mark Proficiency:** As you level up you will gain proficiency in certain skills. If you are proficient in a skill it becomes cheaper to level it up and it gains a bonus from your proficiency bonus. Your proficient bonus starts at +1 and goes up by +1 every 3 levels (Located in the middle of the first table on your character sheet). At level 1 you will also gain Proficiency with 1 Weapon type, for more information see [[2.5 Weapons, Armors, and Tools]]. Remember, you are always proficient with your first, you may not add any bonuses (proficiency or stat) to any weapon attack with a weapon you are not proficient in. For more rulings on proficiency and expertise see [[1.2 Dice and Skills]].
- **Mark Expertise:** As you level up you may gain expertise in certain skills. You must already be proficient to gain expertise in any skill. Expertise and proficiency's effects do stack. If you are an Expert in a skill you gain advantage on skill checks using that skill and leveling up that skill becomes cheaper. 
- **Mark Saving Throws:** Choose two saving throws to be proficient in at level 1, these cannot change and you will never be able to gain more saving throw proficiencies. Saving throws cannot gain expertise and you cannot spend points to raise their modifiers. 
- **Skill Tree:** At level 1 you may put one point into the skill tree (see [[3.3 Leveling Up]]). you will gain the next Skill Tree Level at Level 2. We will come back to this later when we level you up (give you the first level).
- **Languages:** At level 1 you know common and your racial language. If your intelligence is >14 you may learn an additional language for each 2 points in intelligence beyond 14. To see your racial language check your race in the race section. For more information see [[4.2 Languages]].
- **Coinage/Inventory:** You will start with 5 Gold (+-some according to your background and your GM), you may keep this money or spend it shopping before the first session. You may also start with a weapon of your choice, it must be iron tier or lower but does not cost you anything. The normal alternative system would be to roll 1d4 for gold, 1d10+1d6 for silver, and 2d10 for copper as starting money. 
###### Skill Point Cost Chart

| Range | Normal | Proficient | Expert |
| ----- | ------ | ---------- | ------ |
| 1-3   | 1      | 1          | 1      |
| 4-6   | 3      | 1          | 1      |
| 7-9   | 6      | 2          | 1      |
| 10-12 | 9      | 4          | 2      |
| 13-15 | 12     | 6          | 3      |
| 16-18 | 15     | 8          | 4      |
| 19-20 | 18     | 10         | 5      |
> All values within a range cost the listed amount per point. For example, increasing a skill to 7, 8, or 9 costs 6 points for each individual point gained. Skill Points are applied after all other bonuses. Stat Modifiers are calculated first, which means higher base stats increase the cost of improving related skills. Other sources of skill increases (such as proficiency or stat changes) raise the skill value normally and do not increase in cost. The value of each Skill Point depends on when it is allocated.

> **Example**
>
> You want to get good at Athletics. Your Strength is 16 (+3), so your Athletics stat modifier starts at +3. That's your base you don't pay points for that.
>
> Now you want to add points on top. You have 5 Skill Points at level 1. Lets say you put all of them into athletics. You currently don't have proficiency with athletics yet so raising from 3 to 4 is going to cost 3 whole points. Now to go from 4 to 5 it would cost another 3 but you don't have that much. 
> 
> Now lets imagine you do have proficiency. Now raising from 3 to 4 would only cost 1 point, From 4 to 5 another 1, then to 6 for another 1 point. You've now made it higher than before and spent only 3 points. To raise to 7 you'll have to spend 2 points instead and that's all the points you have. 
> 
> Now lets imagine you have expertise. it will only cost 1 point for everything up to 10 so you can spend all 5 points to go to 8 no problem. 
> 
> Now lets imagine you gained +5 to your strength score over the course of some levels. Now you are at 21 (+5) strength. This puts your athletics modifier to a 10 so now even with expertise it will cost 2 to go higher. Raising your stat score will raise the points regardless of cost but still cap at 20.  

#### Step 6: Health, Vitality, and Mana
At level 1 you will have to roll for your health vitality and mana. These will be the dice you use each level. When you gain a Dice in any level you simply roll it and add it to your total.
- **Vitality Dice:** 1d8
- **Health Dice:** 1d6
- **Mana Dice:** 1d6

Once you roll the dice you may add relevant modifiers to your vitality and mana stats. By default you would add your Constitution Modifier to your Vitality roll and your Wisdom Modifier to your Mana roll. Your health gets nothing added to it. 
#### Step 7: Level 1
Up to this point, you have only assigned your base stats. Now you formally become Level 1.

At Level 1, you gain the standard “per-level” bonuses (See [[3.3 Leveling Up]]):

- +2 Skill Points
- +1 Vitality Dice
- +1 Health Dice
- +1 Mana Dice

These are in addition to the values already assigned earlier (including your initial 5 Skill Points and starting dice). This is because your character is treated as having progressed from Level 0 → Level 1.
##### Skill Tree (Level 1)
You also gain 1 Skill Tree Level at Level 1.

- Normally, Skill Tree Levels are gained every 2 levels (2, 4, 6, etc.)
- However, you receive one at Level 1

Choose one of the following paths:

- **Physical Path:** +2 Base Melee Damage and +1 Base Melee Accuracy
- **Magical Path:** You learn a spell, learn the Magic Blast spell, and gain +1 to your generic affinity
- **Utility/Professional Path:** +2 Skill Points
- **Racial Path:** Unlock Tier 1 Racial Ability
- **Pact Path:** Gain access to Tier 1 Pacts

Now you are done building your character and ready to start!


========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/3.0 Character Management/3.2 Character Information.md
========================================================================

## Titles
A title is more than just a name, it’s your character’s reputation, legacy, and identity. Titles are earned through significant achievements or actions that impact a large group of people. They are a mark of honor (or infamy) and can shape how others perceive and interact with your character.
##### Why Have a Title?
Titles serve two main purposes:
1. Convey Authority: Titles like “Lord of Genflen” or “High Priest of Eldath” signify leadership or status.
2. Convey Feats: Titles like “Dragonslayer” or “Hero of Eyum” highlight extraordinary accomplishments.

Titles are given, not chosen, and are often bestowed by deities, rulers, or communities. For example:
- Becoming the ruler of a city might grant you the title “Lord of Genflen.”
- Leading a massacre might earn you the title “Deathbringer.”

Titles can become so well-known that people recognize you more by your title than your name. However, not all titles are positive, infamous titles like “Warlord” or “Betrayer” can lead to fear or hostility.
##### Title Bonuses
Titles often come with small bonuses tied to their nature. These bonuses are determined by the GM and reflect the title’s significance. For example:
- Lord of Genflen: +2 Charisma or advantage on Charisma checks when dealing with subjects of that land.
- Deathbringer: -2 Charisma and advantage on attacks against innocent people.

Bonuses are entirely at the GM’s discretion and should align with the title’s meaning and how it was earned.
##### Self-Assigned Titles
While titles are typically earned, characters can assign themselves titles. However, self-assigned titles only provide bonuses if the character lives up to the title’s expectations. For example:
- A character calling themselves “Hero of Eyum” won’t gain any bonuses until they perform heroic deeds worthy of the title.

Self-assigned titles are more about role-playing and establishing a reputation than providing mechanical benefits.
##### Gods and Titles
Deities often grant titles to mortals as a sign of recognition. These titles are usually bestowed upon clerics, paladins, or other devout followers. Examples include:
- Champion of (God’s Name)
- Harbinger of (God’s Domain)

The bonuses granted by divine titles are limited by the deity’s power. For example, a minor deity might grant a small bonus, while a major deity could provide significant benefits.
##### Multiple Titles
As your character progresses, they may earn multiple titles. You can choose to introduce yourself with any or all of your titles, and you gain the bonuses from all titles. For example:
- A character might be both “Lord of Genflen” and “Dragonslayer,” gaining bonuses from both titles.
##### Losing a Title
Titles can be revoked or replaced if a character fails to live up to them. For example:
- A character with the title “Champion of the People” who begins attacking innocents might lose their title and gain a new one like “False Champion.”
- Revoked titles often come with negative effects, such as penalties to Charisma or disadvantage on social checks.
## Backgrounds
Your background represents your character’s life before the events of the campaign, their career, experiences, and skills. Backgrounds are typically chosen in collaboration with your Game Master (GM) and primarily serve a narrative purpose. While they generally don’t provide mechanical benefits, you and your GM may agree on minor stat boosts or advantages if it fits the story.

**Function of Backgrounds**
The main function of a background is to grant advantage on tasks your character would logically excel at due to their past experiences. For example:
- A Chef would have advantage on cooking-related tasks (if they’ve worked with the ingredients before).
- A Blacksmith would have advantage on crafting or repairing weapons and armor.

GM Discretion: The GM has the final say on whether a background grants advantage. If the GM rules that your background doesn’t apply to a specific situation, you won’t gain advantage.
#### Some Example Backgrounds
| **Name** | **Advantage Scenarios**                                                           | **Example "Pre-Campaign" Life**                         |
| -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Acolyte  | Performing sacred rites, identifying holy relics, or seeking shelter at a temple. | Spent years serving in a temple or cloister.            |
| Criminal | Picking locks, finding black markets, or navigating city sewers.                  | A former cutpurse, smuggler, or low-level enforcer.     |
| Soldier  | Recalling battle tactics, recognizing military rank, or forced marches.           | Served in a local militia or a grand mercenary company. |
| Scholar  | Researching in libraries, translating dead languages, or identifying herbs.       | An apprentice to a wizard or a dedicated bookkeeper.    |
| Artisan  | Appraising the value of goods, repairing tools, or negotiating with merchants.    | A master blacksmith, weaver, or carpenter.              |
| Noble    | Navigating high-court politics, securing an audience with a ruler, or etiquette.  | Born into a family of wealth, power, and land.          |
| Hermit   | Finding clean water in the wild, identifying poisonous plants, or solitude.       | Lived in total isolation on a mountain or in a forest.  |
| Urchin   | Navigating urban mazes, finding "free" food, or hiding in small spaces.           | Grew up on the streets with nothing but your wits.      |
| Sailor   | Navigating by the stars, tying complex knots, or maintaining a vessel.            | Spent your youth on the high seas or merchant cogs.     |
| Laborer  | Heavy lifting, enduring long physical toil, or basic construction.                | A miner, farmhand, or dockworker.                       |
## Karma and Alignment
Karma is a numerical measure of your character's moral standing, ranging from -500 to 500, karma cannot exceed those caps. Your Alignment, Good, Neutral, or Evil, is a label derived directly from your current Karma score. Most creatures begin at 0 Karma with a Neutral Alignment. If it makes sense with your backstory you may optionally take up to -+100 to your Karma score during character creation. 

| Alignment | Karma Threshold |
| --------- | --------------- |
| Evil      | -500 to -250    |
| Neutral   | -249 to 249     |
| Good      | 250 to 500      |

Karma changes when you perform morally significant actions. If a change would push your Karma beyond the cap, it stops at the cap instead. The following actions carry fixed, universal adjustments. Each time you perform the action, apply the change. Your Alignment shifts automatically when your Karma crosses a threshold. If your Karma drops from 60 to -10, your Alignment changes from Good to Neutral accordingly. The world reacts to your Alignment, certain pacts, abilities, and story events key off your Karma score or your Alignment label. 

| Action                                     | Change | Notes                                                                 |
| ------------------------------------------ | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Intentionally kill a non-sentient creature | -1     | This includes all creatures with no alignment or an INT score below 4 |
| Murder a Good-aligned creature             | -5     | Killing in self defense will not reduce your karma                    |
| Murder a Neutral-aligned creature          | -2     | Killing in self defense will not reduce your karma                    |
| Murder an Evil-aligned creature            | +5     | Killing in self defense will not raise your karma                     |
| Steal from a living being                  | -3     | Taking from the dead is not theft and does not affect Karma.          |
| Knowingly lie or manipulate                | -1     | Per significant deception. Minor social niceties do not count.        |
These actions are only a few, many other actions in the Campaign may reduce or raise karma according to the GM's discretion. The only real universal guide is that you must be knowingly committing the action or be at fault for the action in order for it to raise or lower your karma. 


========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/3.0 Character Management/3.3 Leveling Up.md
========================================================================

Level ups will be granted at the GM's digression. Each level you gain will gain you bonuses:

**Every Level** _(1,2,3,4,5,6,7,etc)_
- 2 Skill Points
- 1 Vitality Dice
- 1 Health Dice
- 1 Mana Dice

**Every 2 Levels** _(2,4,6,8,10,etc)_
- 1 Skill Tree Level
- Gain +1 Affinity in Fire, Earth, Water, or Air affinity if you have any levels in Magical path.
- Gain +2 Vitality and +1 Hp if you have any levels in the Physical path.

**Every 3 Levels** _(3,6,9,12,15,etc)_
- 1 Stat Point
- 2 Affinity Points
- 1 Proficiency Upgrade or 2 Skill Points
- Gain 1 Feat
- Gain 1 Spell if you have taken any levels in the Magical path.

> Feats are located under the Skill Trees at the bottom of this section.

**Every 8 Levels** _(8,16,24,etc)_
- 1 Bonus Action Point (BAp)
- 1 Expertise Upgrade (must already be proficient in skill) or 5 Skill Points

**Every 10 Levels** _(10,20,30,etc)_
- 1 Action Point (Ap)

For more information on stats and their caps see [[1.6 Other Rules]] at the bottom section titled "Going Past the Limits With Conversions".
## Skill Tree
Every 2 Levels, you earn 1 Skill Tree Level. This point is used to unlock new paths or progress deeper into your chosen specialties.
##### How to Progress
1. **Unlock the Path (The Root):** You must spend 1 Skill Tree Level to enter a Path (e.g., Physical, Magical). This grants an Initial Bonus.
2. **Unlock the Archetype (The Type):** Once a Path is open, spend your next point to unlock the Level 1 Perk of a specific Archetype (e.g., The Marksman).
3. **Linear Progression:** You must take Level 1 before Level 2, and Level 2 before Level 3 and so on.
4. **Sub-Levels (e.g., Level 1.5, Level 1.75):** These are optional "branches." You do not need them to reach Level 2, but you must have the preceding whole number to take them (e.g., you must have Level 1 to take Level 1.5). If there multiple optional levels on the same level you may take them in any order regardless of numbering (Level 3.5 and Level 3.75 are technically on the same level).
### 1. Physical Path
_Initial Bonus: +2 Base Melee Damage and +1 Base Melee Accuracy_
- **The Indomitable**
- _Each level in this Archetype reduces your Mana Die by one tier (e.g., 1d20 → 1d12 → 1d10, etc). This is retroactive and applies after all other bonuses.  (Sub-Levels do not add to this)_
    - **Level 1:** Vitality Dice is now 1d10 for all future levels.
    - **Level 2:** Vitality Dice is now 1d12 for all future levels.
	    - **Level 2.5:** Your Hit Dice is now 1d8 for all future levels.
    - **Level 3:** Vitality Dice is now 1d20 for all future levels.
	    - **Level 3.5:** Your Hit Dice is now 1d10 for all future levels.
	- **Level 4:** Gain +3 AC
		- **Level 4.5:** Gain +1 AC, you may take this Level up to 5 times.
		- **Level 4.75:** Gain +10 Vitality and +5 Hp, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
- **The Marksman**
    - **Level 1:** +1 Base Ranged Accuracy.
	    - **Level 1.5:** Gain Proficiency in 1 Melee Ranged of your choice, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** Your Ranged attacks ignore Half-Cover.
	    - **Level 2.5:** Gain +1 Base Ranged Accuracy, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
    - **Level 3:** +5 Base Ranged Damage.
	    - **Level 3.5:** Gain Expertise 1 Melee Ranged of your choice which you are already proficient in, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	    - **Level 3.75:** Gain +1 Base Ranged Damage, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
	- **Level 4:** Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged attack when they are threatened by another creature (within melee range) you may add +1d6 to the roll of its damage type.
		- **Level 4.5:** +1d6 damage to ranged advantaged attacks, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
- **The Duelist**
    - **Level 1:** +1 Base Melee Accuracy.
	    - **Level 1.5:** Gain Proficiency in 1 Melee Weapon of your choice, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** +3 Base Melee Damage.
	    - **Level 2.5:** Gain +1 Base Melee Accuracy, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
    - **Level 3:** Once per turn, when you reduce an enemy to 0 HP with a melee attack, you may move up to 10ft and attack a different target for free.
	    - **Level 3.5:** Gain Expertise 1 Melee Weapon of your choice which you are already proficient in, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
	    - **Level 3.75:** Gain +1 Base Melee Damage, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
### 2. Magical Path
_Initial Bonus: You learn a spell, learn the Mana Blast spell, and gain +1 to your generic affinity_
- **The Magician**
- _Each level in this Archetype reduces your Vitality Die by one tier (e.g., 1d20 → 1d12 → 1d10, etc). This is retroactive and applies after all other bonuses. (Sub-Levels do not add to this)_
	- **Level 1:** Your Mana Dice is now 1d8 for all future levels. Gain +1 Generic magic damage. 
		- **Level 1.5:** Instantly gain +2 Affinity Points, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
		- **Level 1.75:** Learn 1 new spell, you may take this Level as many times as you wish
	- **Level 2:** Your Mana Dice is now 1d10 for all future levels.
		- **Level 2.5:** Gain +1 Base Magic Accuracy, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 3:** Your Mana Dice is now 1d12 for all future levels. Gain +1 Generic magic damage. 
		-  **Level 3.5:** Gain +2 Base Magic Damage, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 4:** Your Mana Dice is now 1d20 for all future levels. Gain +2 Generic magic damage. 
		- **Level 4.5:** Gain an additional 2 mana dice this level, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
		- **Level 4.75:** Gain +1 Generic Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
- **The Pyromancer**
    - **Level 1:** You gain +4 Fire Affinity. 
	    - **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 Fire Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** Any spells you cast using the Fire affinity deal +1d4 Fire Damage.
    - **Level 3:** All spells that don't use water affinity will gain +5 Base Magic Accuracy. Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
- **The Geomancer**
    - **Level 1:** You gain +4 Earth Affinity.
	    - **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 Earth Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** Any spells you cast using the Earth affinity deal +1d4 Earth Damage.
    - **Level 3:** All spells that don't use air affinity will gain +5 Base Magic Accuracy. Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
- **The Tidemaster**
    - **Level 1:** You gain +4 Water Affinity. 
	    -  **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 Water Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** Any spells you cast using the Water affinity deal +1d4 Water Damage.
    - **Level 3:** All spells that don't use fire affinity will gain +5 Base Magic Accuracy. Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
- **The Windwalker**
    - **Level 1:** You gain +4 Air Affinity. 
	    -  **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 Air Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** Any spells you cast using the Air affinity deal +1d4 Air Damage.
    - **Level 3:** All spells that don't use earth affinity will gain +5 Base Magic Accuracy Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
- **The Priest**
- *You may not take a level in The Priest unless you have at least 3 points in any of Fire, Earth, Water or Air*
    - **Level 1:** You gain +4 Radiant Affinity. 
	    -  **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 Radiant Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** Any spells you cast using the Radiant affinity deal +1d4 Radiant Damage.
    - **Level 3:** All spells that don't use necrotic affinity will gain +5 Base Magic Accuracy. Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
- **The Necromancer**
- *You may not take a level in The Necromancer unless you have at least 3 points in any of Fire, Earth, Water or Air*
    - **Level 1:** You gain +4 Necrotic Affinity. 
	    -  **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 Necrotic Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
    - **Level 2:** Any spells you cast using the Necrotic affinity deal +1d4 Necrotic Damage.
    - **Level 3:** All spells that don't use radiant affinity will gain +5 Base Magic Accuracy. Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
- **The Healer**
	- **Level 1:** You gain access to Healing spells. See [[6.1 Spells]] for more. 
	- **Level 2:** You have expertise in medicine and +3 to your medicine score.
		- **Level 2.5:** Gain +3 to your medicine score, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- *You may not take any further levels in The Healer if you are of the Evil Alignment*
	- **Level 3:** You may use your Hallowed Affinity instead of Generic affinity when healing. 
		- **Level 3.5:** Gain +2 to your Hallowed Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. You gain +10 Karma. 
	- **Level 4:** Once per short rest, your next healing spell's dice are maximized (treat each die as its maximum value). You gain +1 Hallowed Affinity. 
	- **Level 5:** When you cast a healing spell on a creature below 50% of its max HP, the healing is increased by 50% (round up). You gain +10 Karma. 
	- **Level 6:** Your healing spells cleanse one negative condition from the target. You may activate this a number of times per long rest equal to your Healer Level. You gain +1 Hallowed Affinity. 
	- **Level 7:** When you would be reduced to 0 HP, you may immediately heal yourself for 3d10 + Hallowed Affinity HP. Once per long rest. You gain +10 Karma. 
	- **Level 8:** You may concentrate on two healing spells simultaneously. If you take damage while concentrating on two spells, make a separate concentration save for each. You gain +1 Hallowed Affinity. 
	- **Level 9:** Once per short rest, you may cast a healing spell without expending Mana. You gain +10 Karma. 
	- **Level 10:** When an ally within 60 ft takes damage that would reduce them to 0 HP, you may use your reaction to reduce that damage to 0 instead. Once per long rest. You gain +1 Hallowed 
- **The Jack**
	- **Level 1:** Gain +1 affinity in Fire, Earth, Water, and Air. Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
		- **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 affinity in Fire and Water or Earth and Air, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 2:** Gain +1 affinity in Radiant, Necrotic, and Psychic.
		- **Level 2.5:** Gain +1 in either Radiant and Necrotic or +2 in Psychic affinity.
	- **Level 3:** Gain +3 Affinity Points this level and +1 on every subsequent level. Gain +3 Generic magic damage. 
### 3. Utility & Professional
_Initial Bonus: +2 Skill Points_
- **Scholar**
    - **Level 1:** Gain an additional 1 Skill Point on all future levels (if you are over level 5 when you gain this perk instantly gain +10 Skill Points).
    - **Level 2:** Gain an extra proficiency (or 3 Skill Points) each time you gain an proficiency.
    - **Level 3:** Gain an extra expertise (or 5 Skill Points) each time you gain an expertise.
	    - **Level 3.5:** Instantly gain +2 Skill Points and +1 Stat Point, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 4:** Gain an extra feat whenever you gain a feat.
- **Scoundrel**
    - **Level 1:** Gain +2 to Initiative, you have advantage on your first attack during the first round of combat.
	    - **Level 1.5:** You may sprint, hide, or sneak as a bonus action instead of an action.
    - **Level 2:** During the first round of initiative all attacks gain +1d6 damage of their respective type. 
    - **Level 3:** Gain +30 movement speed and +1 Ap on the first round of initiative. 
	    - **Level 3.5:** Gain +1 Initiative, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 4:** You have advantage on all attacks during the first round of combat. 
- **Rune Master**
	- **Level 1:** You can now use/activate runes. For more information see [[6.2 Runes]]
	- **Level 2:** Decrease the mana cost of runes by -10% (rounding up), apply this modifier after the Surcharge Cost + Rune Cost.
		- *The following Sub-levels must be taken in order.*
		- **Level 2.5:** Saving throws caused from your runes gain +5 to their DC.
		- **Level 2.75:** Saving throws caused from your runes gain +2 to their DC, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 3:** +2 Bonus on Runic DC Checks.
		- **Level 3.5:** +1 on Runic DC Checks, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
	- **Level 4:** Decrease runic circle level requirements by half (rounding up) and surcharge mana cost by half (rounding up).
	- **Level 5:** You can make your own runes, see [[6.2 Runes]] for more details. 
	- **Level 6:** If an inscription check fails, one Carbon rune shatters to protect the rest, you may retry the failed rune once without losing the circle but must replace a carbon rune.
- **Monster Specialist**
	- **Slime Rancher**
	- *You may collect up to 1d4 jars of slime per day from a slime without injuring it.*
		- **Level 1:** When you encounter a slime you may force it to make a Wis Save (DC 10 + Affinity of Slime), on a failed save it becomes docile and will no longer attack you unless you attack it first. 
			- **Level 1.5:** You gain +1 Gel affinity (bypassing normal affinity requirements), you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
		- **Level 2:** You may force two slimes to make a Con Save (DC = 10 + Affinity of Slime) to breed making new slimes. For more information on slime breeding see [[7.3.1 Slimes]].
		- **Level 3:** When you make a check to harvest slime you may use 1d6 instead of 1d4.
			- **Level 3.5:** You may now tame slimes of half your level or lower to become your Familiar (see [[1.5 Mounts and Familiars]]).
		- **Level 4:** Your slime sells for 10% more. For slime prices and drop tables see [[2.6 Materials]].
		- **Level 5:** You may force slimes of different types to breed, the DC check will use both affinities. 
	- **Golem Tamer**
	- *Golems will not attack you unless you attack them first.*
		- **Level 1:** You may give one word commands to golems who are not aggressive to you which they must follow. If the command has the potential to endanger the golem it can resist your command with a Wis Save (DC 5). Only one golem will listen to you at once. If your command would directly lead to a Golem's death (such as die) it will automatically refuse. 
		- **Level 2:** You may overcharge a Golem with your own mana and force it to reproduce on its own. See [[7.3.2 Golems]] for more information on how to breed Golems. 
			- **Level 2.5:** If you feed a Golem up to it's maximum x3 overcharge it will raise the Golem by one level and reduce it's mana to 0. 
		- **Level 3:** The Wis Save for resisting your commands DC raises to 15. You may now command up to two golems. 
			- **Level 3.5:** You may now tame Golems of 1/3 your level or lower to become your Familiar (see [[1.5 Mounts and Familiars]]).
		- **Level 4:** You may force Golems to breed via Fusion to create new types of golems. You may now command up to three golems. 
		- **Level 5:** You may harvest 30% more material (up to 95%) from a golem when it is killed. For golem drop tables see [[2.6 Materials]]. 
		- **Level 6:** You may command any Golem that is at least 5 levels below you to die or drop and it will release all of its material (material loss rates still apply) as if it was dead, you may then feed the core mana to bring it back sustainably. This action will not cause the Golem to be aggressive towards you. 
	- **Dragonslayer**
	- *You gain 1d8 + Relevant Affinity damage mitigation from attacks from dragons. For more information about dragons see [[7.3.3 Dragons]].*
		- **Level 1:** You may add your relevant affinity modifier to all damage rolls against dragons.
		- **Level 2:** You gain lesser resistance to attacks from dragons of any type which you have greater than 5 affinity in. This resistance applies after regular resistances and cannot be pierced. 
		- **Level 3:** Dragons have disadvantage to hit you.
		- **Level 4:** You gain resistance to attacks from dragons of any type which you have greater than 10 affinity in. This resistance applies after regular resistances and cannot be pierced. 
		- **Level 5:** Dragons will not be aggressive to you (or one of your allies) until you show aggression (make an attack) against them.
			- *The following Sub-levels must be taken in order.*
			- **Level 5.1:** You learn how to speak Dragonic and can communicate freely with dragons. 
			- **Level 5.2:** Dragons will no longer assume your allies are with you if your allies attack the dragon and will remain docile to you even if attacked by an ally.
			- **Level 5.3:** You may harvest roughly 1 lbs/level of scales from a dragon who is not aggressive to you without provoking it once per week. The scales will grow back after a week and this will not harm the dragon. You can only do this to dragons who are half your level or lower. For dragon scale drop tables see [[2.6 Materials]]. 
			- **Level 5.4:** Dragons of half your level or lower can now be used as companions/pets and will follow your commands (see [[1.5 Mounts and Familiars]]). 
			- **Level 5.5:** Dragons will treat you as one of their own and try to protect you (GM's digression). This effect only applies to dragons which are at half your level or lower. 
			- **Level 5.6:** You may now tame Dragons of 1/5 your level or lower to become your Familiar (see [[1.5 Mounts and Familiars]]).
		- **Level 6:** When you roll above 15 on an attack roll against a dragon you deal max damage. If the dragon fails a saving throw from you on a damage dealing effect by more than 10 it will take max damage. 
		- **Level 7:** You gain greater resistance to attacks from dragons of any type which you have greater than 15 affinity in. This resistance applies after regular resistances and cannot be pierced. 
		- **Level 8:** You gain advantage on attacks against all dragons.
		- **Level 9:** You deal double damage against all dragons and gain automatically succeed any saving throw inflicted from a dragons attack/effect. 
	- **Monster Hunter**
	- *You deal +1 damage against all monsters (non humanoids)*
		- **Level 1:** You deal +1 damage against all monsters (non humanoids), you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
		- **Level 2:** Monsters (non humanoids) have -1 to attack rolls against you, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
	- **Human Hunter**
	- *You deal +1 damage against all humanoids, you may not take this level unless you are of an Evil alignment*
		- **Level 1:** You deal +1 damage against all humanoids, you may take this Level as many times as you wish.
		- **Level 2:** Humanoids have -1 to attack rolls against you.
### 4. Racial Path
_Initial Bonus: Unlock Tier 1 Racial Ability_
- **Bloodline Evolution**
    - **Level 1:** Unlock Tier 2 Racial Ability
    - **Level 2:** Unlock Tier 3 Racial Ability.
    - **Level 3:** Unlock Tier 4 Racial Ability.
    - **Level 4:** Unlock Tier 5 Racial Ability.
    - **Level 5:** Unlock Tier 6 Racial Ability.
    - **Level 6:** Unlock Tier 7 Racial Ability.
    - **Level 7:** Unlock Tier 8 Racial Ability.
    - **Level 8:** Unlock Tier 9 Racial Ability.
    - **Level 9:** Unlock Tier 10 Racial Ability.
- **General Evolution**
	- **Level 1:** Gain 30 ft of Darkvision (you can see in the dark but only in black and white).
		- *The following Sub-levels must be taken in order.*
		- **Level 1.1:** Your Darkvision is now 60 ft.
		- **Level 1.2:** Your Darkvision is now 90 ft.
		- **Level 1.3:** Your Darkvision can now see through magical darkness.
		- **Level 1.4:** Your Darkvision can now see in color.
	- **Level 2:** Gain +1 to initiative.
	- **Level 3:** You are immune to the Threatened condition. 
	- **Level 4:** You are immune to the Surprised condition and gain +3 to initiative. 
	- **Level 5:** Your movement speed increases by +10 ft.
		- **Level 5.5:** Your movement speed increases by 5 ft, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 6:** You gain an additional Skill Tree Level every level when you would not normally gain one (levels 3,5,7, etc).
### 5. Pact Path
_Initial Bonus: Access to Low-Level Pacts (You may make pacts with any deity of any type at level 1 of the Pact Path but you will only have access to their Tier 1 Pacts)_
- **The Pact Taker**
	- **Level 1:** You may form pacts with deities with whom you have already failed 3+ pacts with.
	- **Level 2:** Pact periods are extended by 20% (rounding down to the day with a minimum bonus of +1 day).
	- **Level 3:** The Punishment Period from failing a pact is reduced by 50% (rounding down to the day with a minimum bonus of -1 day).
	- **Level 4:** You may remove 1 permanent punishment you have as a consequence from failing a pact, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
- **The Beacon**
- *Form pacts with Gods of good. If you take a level in The Beacon you may not take levels in The Reaper.*
    - **Level 1:** Gain access to Tier 2 pacts with Gods.
    - **Level 2:** Gain access to Tier 3 pacts with Gods.
    - **Level 3:** Gain access to Tier 4 pacts with Gods.
    - **Level 4:** Gain access to Tier 5 pacts with Gods.
- **The Occultist**
- *Form pacts with Ancient neutral beings*
    - **Level 1:** Gain access to Tier 2 pacts with Ancients.
    - **Level 2:** Gain access to Tier 3 pacts with Ancients.
    - **Level 3:** Gain access to Tier 4 pacts with Ancients.
    - **Level 4:** Gain access to Tier 5 pacts with Ancients.
- **The Reaper**
- *Form pacts with evil fiendish creatures. If you take a level in The Reaper you may not take levels in The Beacon.*
    - **Level 1:** Gain access to Tier 2 pacts with Fiends.
    - **Level 2:** Gain access to Tier 3 pacts with Fiends.
    - **Level 3:** Gain access to Tier 4 pacts with Fiends.
    - **Level 4:** Gain access to Tier 5 pacts with Fiends.
- **The Eldritch Horror**
- *Use otherworldly power to make yourself stronger. If you take a level in Eldritch Horror you may no longer take levels in The Beacon, The Occultist, or The Reaper and all levels previously taken in them will be lost.*
	- **Level 1:** Learn the spell Eldritch Blast. You lose 20 karma. 
		- **Level 1.5:** Gain +2 Eldritch Affinity, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. You lose 1 karma. 
	- **Level 2:** Your Eldritch Blast deals 3 damage instead of 1. Your veins glow a faint purple under your skin while using magic. You lose 1 karma. 
		- **Level 2.5:** Gain +20 ft of True Sight and +5 ft movement speed. You lose 1 karma. 
	- **Level 3:** Your Eldritch Blast's range increases by 60 ft. Your eyes glow a faint purple while using magic. You lose 1 karma. 
		- **Level 3.5:** Eldritch Affinity can now be upgraded with Affp. You lose 1 karma. 
	- **Level 4:** Your Eldritch Blast now costs 1 mana. You may learn 1 spell.  You lose 3 karma. 
	- **Level 5:** Learn the spell Eldritch Explosion. Your skin becomes cold to the touch at all times. You lose 10 karma. 
		- *The following Sub-levels must be taken in order.*
		- **Level 5.1:** Gain +20 ft fly speed (dark energy surrounds you and lifts you). You lose 5 karma. 
		- **Level 5.2:** Gain +20 ft fly speed. Creatures within 10 ft of you while you are flying have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You lose 5 karma. 
		- **Level 5.3:** Learn the spell Eldritch Tentacle. You lose 5 karma. 
		- **Level 5.4:** Gain +5 ft fly speed, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. You lose 5 karma. 
	- *You may not take levels beyond this point if you are of a good alignment. If you are of the good alignment you must do evil acts to lower your Karma first.*
	- **Level 6:** You may spend a Bonus Action (BAp) with any attack (not aoe) to infuse it with Eldritch Energy which changes the damage type and affinity (for spells) to Eldritch. On hit target gains the Eldritch Curse condition for 2 turns.  You lose 3 karma. 
		- **Level 6.5:** When targets gain the Eldritch Curse condition from one of your attacks they also take 5 eldritch damage at the start of each turn. You lose 3 karma. 
	- **Level 7:** Your Eldritch Blast deals 8 damage instead of 3. You become thinner and more frail looking, your eyes become sunken and hollow, lose 1 to your STR and CON scores. You lose 3 karma. 
	- **Level 8:** Your Eldritch Blast fires 3 beams as a base instead of 1. You may learn 1 spell. Your voice becomes frail and scratchy, lose 1 to your CHAR score. You lose 3 karma. 
	- **Level 9:** You may choose which creatures are or aren't affected by your Eldritch Explosion spell whom are in the radius. Your shadows lag behind your movements be a second. You lose 10 karma. 
	- *You may not take levels beyond this point if you are of a good/neutral alignment. If you are of the good/neutral alignment you must do evil acts to lower your Karma first.*
	- **Level 10:** The Unknown One has noticed your presence. You have advantage on Intimidation checks and gain +5 to Intimidation. All creatures who have pacts with deities will view you as a monster/danger who should be killed. You can no longer be resurrected by traditional means. You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 11:** Your Eldritch spells deal double damage. Gain +5 Eldritch Affinity. You may learn 1 spell. You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 12:** When you deal more than 20 Eldritch damage at once with a single attack the target must make a STR save (DC 10 + Eldritch Affinity) or be knocked prone. You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 13:** When you inflict Eldritch Curse the duration is doubled and it now does 5 eldritch damage at the start of each turn (if you have Level 6.5 damage is increased to 20). You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 14:** Learn the spell Eldritch Shadow. Damage doubling from Eldritch Shadow stacks with Level 11. You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 15:** The Unknown One is pleased with your performance. Whenever you would deal Eldritch damage to Vitality you may choose to deal half of that damage to Health. You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 16:** When you die you will come back to life after 24 hours. Your body will regenerate and look the same as you did before but slightly more frail. You are now considered an undead creature. You no longer age. As an undead you gain +5 to your Necrotic and Eldritch Affinities and when you would normally make a roll with your Eldritch Affinity you may also add your Necrotic affinity. You may resurrect once every week, if you die again during the cool down period you are dead permanently. As an undead you suffer a -10 penalty on persuasion rolls against creatures of a good or Neutral alignment. You lose 25 karma. 
	- **Level 17:** You may now add your Generic Affinity to things which normally use Eldritch Affinity only. You may learn 1 spell. You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 18:** When you deal Eldritch Damage the damage is doubled (stacks with Levels 11 and 14). You lose 10 karma. 
	- **Level 19:** Your race is now considered a Lich (applies like a racial modifier on top of your race). If you have made it to this point without dying you are now considered an undead and immediately gain +10 Eldritch Affinity (you do not gain this bonus if you have resurrected before). You may now resurrect once a day instead of once a week and resurrection takes 3 hours instead of 24. You lose 25 karma. 
	- **Level 20:** The Unknown One considers you one of his own. You are now capable of dealing damage to deities. All creatures will view you as a monster/danger which needs to be killed. You are considered the pinnacle of Absolute Evil. You lose 50 karma. When you deal Eldritch Damage the damage is doubled (stacks with Levels 11, 14, and 18). You may change the Affinity and Damage type of any spell you know into Eldritch. You may add the absolute value of your Karma (your karma but positive) to any damage roll of the eldritch damage type at the cost of an action (Ap). 
- **The Godslayer**
- *Requires at least 5 levels in The Eldritch Horror.*
	- **Level 1:** You can now deal damage to Deities. All Deities have greater resistance to your damage. 
	- **Level 2:** You have +10 to hit against Deities.
		- **Level 2.5:** You gain an additional +5 to hit against Deities, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 3:** All Deities have resistance to your damage. 
	- **Level 4:** You gain +10 to saving throws inflicted from Deities. 
	- **Level 5:** All Deities have lesser resistance to your damage. 
	- **Level 6:** You have lesser resistance to all damage dealt from Deities.
	- **Level 7:** All Deities now take full damage from you. 
	- **Level 8:** You deal +5 damage to Deities.
		- **Level 8.5:** You deal +3 damage to Deities, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 9:** All Deities have disadvantage on saving throws imposed by you and the DC of those rolls raises by +5.
		- **level 9.5:** The DC raises by an additional +3, you may take this Level as many times as you wish. 
	- **Level 10:** You deal double damage to Deities and have advantage on attack rolls against them. 
## Feats
A feat is a special ability you gain as you level up. 

Types of Feats: 
- Passive: A feat that is always active
- Conditional: A passive feat which is only active during some conditions
- Active: An active use ability or skill, these can be actions, bonus actions, reactions, or free actions

> For weapon and armor references see [[2.5 Weapons, Armors, and Tools]]. For condition references see [[2.4 Conditions]]. For affinity references see [[2.1 Affinities]].

| Name                | Type         | Effect/Description                                                                                                                                                                      | Prerequisites                        |
| ------------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| Quick Draw          | Passive      | You can stow and draw weapons as a Free Action.                                                                                                                                         | Dex 12                               |
| Toughness           | Passive      | Gain +1 HP and +1 Vit per level (Retroactive).                                                                                                                                          | None                                 |
| Marathon Runner     | Passive      | Increase base Speed by +5ft.                                                                                                                                                            | Con 12                               |
| Grappler            | Passive      | You have advantage on Athletics checks to grapple. Creatures that you have grappled take double damage from your attacks.                                                               | Str 14                               |
| Brutal Critical     | Passive      | Your critical hits deal an additional +1d8 damage.                                                                                                                                      | Level 6                              |
| Brawler             | Passive      | Your unarmed strikes damage go up by a dice tier, you may take this Feat up to 3 times.                                                                                                 | Str 14                               |
| Distant Shot        | Passive      | Your Ranged weapon's maximum range is increased by 50%.                                                                                                                                 | Marksman Lvl 2                       |
| Eagle Eye           | Passive      | You can see clearly up to 1 mile; +2 to Search and Spot checks.                                                                                                                         | Wis 12                               |
| Weapon Master       | Passive      | Choose 1 weapon type; gain +1 Accuracy with it.                                                                                                                                         | Proficient in Weapon                 |

| Mana Flow           | Passive      | Increase Max Mana by +2 per level (retroactive).                                                                                                                                        | Magician Lvl 1                       |
| Spell Sniper        | Passive      | Double the range of your spells.                                                                                                                                                        | Magician Lvl 1                       |
| Stone Skin          | Passive      | Increase Earth Affinity by +2; gain +1 AC.                                                                                                                                              | Geomancer Lvl 1                      |

| Fast Hands          | Passive      | You gain a Bonus Action Point.                                                                                                                                                          | Scoundrel Lvl 2                      |
| Jack of All Trades  | Passive      | Add half Proficiency Bonus to skills you aren't proficient in.                                                                                                                          | Scholar Lvl 3                        |
| Medic               | Passive      | Gain advantage on medicine checks and advantage on dice which heal.                                                                                                                     | Medicine Prof.                       |
| Dungeon Delver      | Passive      | Advantage on finding traps and Lesser Resistance to damage from traps.                                                                                                                  | None                                 |
| Trader              | Passive      | Gain Expertise in Barter skill and +3 to Barter rolls.                                                                                                                                  | Charisma 12                          |
| Observant           | Passive      | +5 to passive Search and Spot rolls.                                                                                                                                                    | Wisdom 14                            |
| Master of Disguise  | Passive      | Advantage on Deception checks to pass as someone else.                                                                                                                                  | Scoundrel Lvl 1                      |
| Historian           | Passive      | When you fail a History check, you can reroll it once.                                                                                                                                  | Scholar Lvl 1                        |
| Acrobat             | Passive      | You take half damage from falling and gain proficiency in Acrobatics skill.                                                                                                             | Dex 14                               |
| Iron Will           | Passive      | Gain +4 to Wisdom Saving Throws.                                                                                                                                                        | Level 9                              |
| Forbidden Knowledge | Passive      | Gain +5 to Arcana and +3 to Generic Affinity but -2 to Social Insight.                                                                                                                  | Occultist Lvl 1                      |
| Darkness Seer       | Passive      | You can see through magical and non-magical darkness up to 60 ft.                                                                                                                       | Reaper Lvl 2                         |
| Giant's Strength    | Passive      | Your Size is considered one tier larger for carrying/lifting/grappling.                                                                                                                 | Str 18                               |
| Cat’s Grace         | Passive      | You never take damage from falls under 30ft and are no longer knocked prone from falling.                                                                                               | Dex 18                               |
| Hardened Soul       | Passive      | You are immune to the Frightened, Despair, and Demoralized conditions.                                                                                                                  | Level 12                             |
| Eternal Mana        | Passive      | If you are at below 10 mana gain 2 mana. This can activate once every 6 seconds.                                                                                                        | Magician Lvl 3                       |
| Titan’s Grip        | Passive      | You can hold a Two-Handed weapon in one hand and duel wield with it. You gain all bonuses from duel wielding and from two handed weapons while you do this.                             | Str 20                               |
| Unstoppable Force   | Passive      | You are immune to slowed and paralyzed conditions.                                                                                                                                      | Indomitable Lvl 3                    |
| Rounded Mage        | Passive      | You may choose two affinities which are the same amount to be linked, whenever you gain affinity in one you also gain it in the other.                                                  | >3 affinity in at least 2 affinities |
| All Rounded Mage    | Passive      | You may link a third affinity, it follows the same rules as Rounded Mage.                                                                                                               | Rounded Mage                         |
| Affinity Master     | Passive      | Instantly gain +2 affinity in Fire, Earth Water, and air affinities and +3 Affinity Points (Affp)                                                                                       | None                                 |
| Dodge Master        | Passive      | Your max AC bonus from Dex raises by +3 up to a max of +7.                                                                                                                              | Dex 22                               |
| Karma Hoarder       | Passive      | When you would gain or lose karma from an action you gain or lose twice as much.                                                                                                        | None                                 |
| Silver Tongue       | Passive      | If you roll below a 10 on any Persuasion, Deception, or Intimidation roll you may reroll. You must use the new result.                                                                  | Charisma 14                          |
| Still Mind          | Passive      | You gain +5 to concentration checks and advantage on those rolls.                                                                                                                       | Wis 14                               |
| Pact Tactics        | Conditional  | While you and an ally are both threatening the same creature you gain +3 to all attack rolls.                                                                                           | None                                 |
| Armor Training      | Conditional  | While wearing heavy armor gain +3 AC or medium armor gain +2 AC.                                                                                                                        | Str 13, Medium Armor Proficency      |
| Heavy Hitter        | Conditional  | When using a two-handed weapon, add +5 to Melee Damage.                                                                                                                                 | Str 14                               |
| Point Blank Shot    | Conditional  | You do not have disadvantage on Ranged attacks within 5ft of an enemy.                                                                                                                  | Marksman Lvl 1                       |
| Great Cleave        | Conditional  | If you kill an enemy you may make another attack as a free action, this attack this turn deals +3 damage. This may only be done once per turn.                                          | Str 16                               |
| Shield Master       | Conditional  | Double the AC bonus provided by your shield.                                                                                                                                            | Str 12                               |
| Steady Aim          | Conditional  | If you don't move this turn, gain +2 Accuracy on your next shot.                                                                                                                        | Marksman Lvl 1                       |
| Dual Wielder        | Conditional  | While wielding two weapons at once gain +2 to all attack rolls using those weapons                                                                                                      | Dex 14                               |
| Mobile              | Conditional  | After you make a melee attack, you don't provoke reactions from that target for this round.                                                                                             | Speed 30+                            |
| Charging Strike     | Conditional  | If you move at least 15ft in a straight line to melee attack; deal +1d6 bludgeoning damage.                                                                                             | Str 14                               |
| Endurance           | Conditional  | When reduced to 0 HP, drop to 1 HP instead, once per combat.                                                                                                                            | Indomitable Lvl 1                    |
| Disarming Attack    | Conditional  | When you make a melee attack target must pass a Str Save (DC 10+StrMod) or drop their weapon. When the target fails the check they take an additional 1d4 slashing damage.              | Str 14                               |
| Trip Attack         | Conditional  | When you make a melee attack target must pass a Dex Save (DC 10+StrMod) or fall Prone.                                                                                                  | Dex 14                               |
| Vital Spot          | Conditional  | Deal +1d12 damage to targets that are Prone or Restrained.                                                                                                                              | Intelligence 12                      |
| Executioner         | Conditional  | Melee attacks against creatures with less than 10% HP are always Crits.                                                                                                                 | Duelist Lvl 3                        |
| Save or Don't Suck  | Conditional  | Spells that deal 0 damage on a save now deal half damage.                                                                                                                               | Magician Lvl 2                       |
| Firebrand           | Conditional  | If your attack spell is Fire Affinity targets must make a Con Save (DC 10+FireAffinity) or gain On Fire condition.                                                                      | Pyromancer Lvl 1                     |
| Flowing Water       | Conditional  | Move 5ft without provoking reactions after casting a spell with Water Affinity.                                                                                                         | Tidemaster Lvl 1                     |
| Wind Rush           | Conditional  | If your attack spell is Air Affinity targets must make a Strength Save (DC 10+AirAffinity) or be pushed 5 ft away from you.                                                             | Windwalker Lvl 1                     |
| Soul Reaper         | Conditional  | Gain Mana equal to half of damage dealt with final blow when you kill a living creature with Necrotic.                                                                                  | Necromancer Lvl 1                    |
| Static Discharge    | Conditional  | If you take damage, your next Lightning spell deals +1d6.                                                                                                                               | Lightning Aff 3+                     |
| Vampiric Link       | Conditional  | Heal 2 HP every time you deal Necrotic damage.                                                                                                                                          | Necromancer Lvl 2                    |
| Mind Spike          | Passive      | Psychic spells deal half of their damage to HP.                                                                                                                                         | Psychic Aff 5+                       |
| Explosive Power     | Conditional  | Your spells deal +5 force damage if they hit 3 or more targets.                                                                                                                         | Magician Lvl 3                       |
| Lingering Shadow    | Conditional  | Necrotic spells leave a 5ft cloud that deals 5 Necrotic Damage for 1 round where they land at the start of targets turn.                                                                | Necromancer Lvl 3                    |
| Gravity Well        | Conditional  | Earth spells pull the target (If they fail a DC 10+EarthAff Str Save) 5ft toward the center of the impact (target must be within 20 ft).                                                | Geomancer Lvl 2                      |
| Infiltrator         | Conditional  | You have double advantage on Stealth checks in dim light.                                                                                                                               | Scoundrel Lvl 1                      |
| Cursed Blood        | Conditional  | When hit by melee, the attacker takes 1d4 Necrotic dmg.                                                                                                                                 | Reaper Lvl 1                         |
| 1,000 Paper Cuts    | Conditional  | Deal +15 true damage to creatures who's max health is higher than double your max health.                                                                                               | Level 10                             |
| Apex Predator       | Conditional  | If you are first in initiative, your first attack is a guaranteed crit.                                                                                                                 | Scoundrel Lvl 3                      |
| Defensive Duelist   | Active (Rp)  | Add your Proficiency Bonus to AC against one melee attack.                                                                                                                              | Duelist Lvl 1                        |
| Sentinel            | Active (Rp)  | When a creature within 5ft attacks an ally, you can attack that creature.                                                                                                               | Str 12                               |
| Parry               | Active (Rp)  | Reduce incoming melee damage by your Str or Dex Modifier.                                                                                                                               | Duelist Lvl 1                        |
| Second Wind         | Active (BAp) | Heal HP equal to your Hit Dice once per combat.                                                                                                                                         | Con 14                               |
| Hunker Down         | Active (BAp) | Skip your turn to gain +6 AC until the start of your next turn and +1d6 Vitality; Must be used at the start of your turn.                                                               | Indomitable Lvl 2                    |
| Elemental Shield    | Active (BAp) | Gain Resistance to a damage type that you have at least 5 affinity in until the start of your next turn.                                                                                | Any Affinity 5+                      |
| Mana Well           | Active (BAp) | Recover 1d10 Mana, once per combat.                                                                                                                                                     | Magician Lvl 2                       |
| Arcane Overload     | Active (BAp) | Next spell cast deals Max Damage; take 1d10 True damage.                                                                                                                                | Magician Lvl 4                       |
| Divinely Favored    | Active (BAp) | Reroll one d20 roll. Once per Long Rest.                                                                                                                                                | Beacon Lvl 1                         |
| Overdrive           | Active (BAp) | Gain +1 Ap this turn but lose your BAp next turn (and costs 1 BAp this turn).                                                                                                           | Level 15                             |
| Demonic Bargain     | Active (BAp) | Sacrifice 10 Vitality or 5 HP to gain 20 Mana.                                                                                                                                          | Reaper Lvl 3                         |
| Vault               | Active (Ap)  | Move 10ft over an obstacle or enemy without an Athletics check or penalties to movement.                                                                                                | Dex 14                               |
| Blessed Touch       | Active (Ap)  | Heal a targets Vitality by 1d4 + Radiant Affinity.                                                                                                                                      | Priest Lvl 1                         |
| Mana Burn           | Active (Ap)  | Target must make a Wis Save (DC 10+GenericAff) on fail they take force damage equal to half their current Mana.                                                                         | Intelligence 18                      |
| Quick Spells        | Active       | Change a spell's casting time from 1 Ap to 1 BAp for a attack. This may only be done once every 3 rounds of combat.                                                                     | Magician Lvl 3                       |
| Twin Cast           | Active       | Spend double Mana to hit two targets with a single-target spell.                                                                                                                        | Magician Lvl 3                       |
