Those who succeed take half damage. You must, once per day, make an attack that puts yourself in danger (provoke an opportunity attack, attack from unstable ground, etc.). Failure results in losing your bonus action each turn for 4 days.

**Tier 4 (8 days):** Once per long rest, you may enter Overdrive for 1 minute. While Overdrive, you take two turns on your first round of combat, your melee attacks deal double damage, and creatures within 15 ft of you take 2d10 Fire damage at the start of your turns. You must personally defeat (reduce to 0 HP) at least 3 hostile creatures during the pact period. Failure results in Exhaustion (3 stacks) that cannot be removed for 8 days.

**Tier 5 (20 days):** You gain all Ignivar Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may create a 40 ft radius Nova Pulse centered on yourself. All creatures must make a CON save (DC 16) or take 10d10 Fire damage and be Stunned for 1 turn. Those who succeed take half damage. You are immune to this effect. You must, before the pact ends, lead a charge into battle or be the first to engage in at least 3 separate combats. Failure results in permanent Greater Weakness to Fire and Cold damage.

## Aegirion
*Water*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Water damage. The weight of water aids you: all creatures within 30 ft treat each foot of movement as 2 ft (cumulative with difficult terrain). You are immune to this effect. You must, once per day, sign or write a binding agreement about something (a promise, a plan, a trade). Failure results in your speed being halved for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** Once per short rest, as a bonus action, you may call a wave that sweeps a 30 ft cone. Creatures must make a STR save (DC 13) or be pushed 20 ft away from you and take 3d10 Water damage. You must, once per day, wait at least 10 minutes before making a decision in a stressful situation. Failure results in you being unable to take the Dash or Disengage actions for 4 days.

**Tier 3 (7 days):** At the start of each of your turns, you regain Vitality equal to your level if you are standing in or adjacent to water. If you are not near water, you regain half that amount once per hour out of combat. You must, by the end of the pact, construct or repair something that will last (a wall, a contract framework, a relationship). Failure results in you being unable to regain Vitality from any source for 7 days.

**Tier 4 (12 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you create a 40 ft radius Pressure Zone centered on yourself for 1 minute. Creatures inside must make a CON save (DC 15) at the start of their turns or take 4d10 Bludgeoning damage and be Restrained for that turn. You must, once per day, convince someone to change their mind through calm discussion (not intimidation). Failure results in you gaining the Restrained condition at the start of each combat for 12 days until you take damage.

**Tier 5 (25 days):** You gain all Aegirion Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may flood a 100 ft radius with waist-deep water for 1 hour. While in the water, you may teleport to any point within it as a bonus action, and non-allied creatures have their speed halved. You must, by the end of the pact, establish a lasting agreement between two hostile parties. Failure results in permanent half movement speed in all terrain.

## Eldathis
*Radiant*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Radiant damage. Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature that has lied to you or broken a promise, you deal +2d10 Radiant damage. You must, once per day, speak a hard truth that costs you something (a relationship, a reputation, an opportunity). Failure results in the Cursed condition for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** Once per short rest, as a bonus action, you may detect lies within 30 ft for 10 minutes. Any creature that speaks a deliberate lie while you are concentrating glows faintly and you know it is lying. You must, once per day, judge someone publicly (call out a wrongdoing, report a crime, or enforce a rule). Failure results in you being unable to speak anything but the truth for 4 days — any attempt to lie deals 2d10 Psychic damage to you.

**Tier 3 (8 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you may fire a 120 ft line of Radiant light (10 ft wide). Creatures must make a WIS save (DC 14) or take 6d10 Radiant damage. Creatures that are Cursed, Withered, or Corrupted take double damage. You must, once per day, make a public atonement for a wrong you have done (apologize, return stolen goods, confess). Failure results in the Cursed condition and disadvantage on all CHA checks for 8 days.

**Tier 4 (14 days):** Once per long rest, you may activate Aura of Judgment for 1 minute. Creatures within 30 ft that tell a lie, break an oath, or act against their stated alignment must make a WIS save (DC 14) or take 5d10 Radiant damage and gain the Cursed condition for 24 hours. You must, by the end of the pact, successfully resolve a moral conflict where you choose the harder right over the easier wrong. Failure results in you being Cursed permanently and unable to benefit from any divine blessing for 14 days.

**Tier 5 (30 days):** You gain all Eldathis Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may declare Final Judgment on one creature you can see within 120 ft. It must make a WIS save (DC 16). On a failure, it takes 15d10 Radiant damage and is Stunned for 1 turn. On a success, it takes half damage. If the creature is below half Health, the damage is doubled. You must, by the end of the pact, convert at least 10 people to follow a moral code or law you uphold. Failure results in permanent Despair and you cannot be healed by Radiant or Holy effects.

## Cryovara
*Ice/Cold*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Cold damage. Creatures within 15 ft of you take 1d10 Cold damage at the start of their turns. You must, once per day, endure the cold for 10 minutes without shelter (or if cold is not available, endure some other discomfort like a cold bath). Failure results in the Frozen (2 stacks) condition at the start of each day for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** You gain 20 temporary armor at the start of each combat. This armor absorbs damage before your Vitality. You must, once per day, go without food for a full meal (skip one meal). Failure results in your armor being permanently reduced by 5 for 4 days (minimum 0).

**Tier 3 (7 days):** The ground within 20 ft of you becomes slippery ice. Non-allied creatures must make a DEX save (DC 13) at the start of their turns or fall Prone. You move across this ice at full speed. You must, once per day, prepare for a future threat (stock supplies, reinforce shelter, plan an escape route). Failure results in you being knocked Prone at the start of each combat for 7 days.

**Tier 4 (12 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you unleash a 60 ft cone of freezing wind. Creatures must make a CON save (DC 15) or take 6d10 Cold damage and gain Frozen (4 stacks). Those who succeed take half damage and gain Frozen (2 stacks). You must, once per day, complete a task that requires patience (wait in line for 30+ minutes, perform a repetitive chore for an hour, etc.). Failure results in Frozen (5 stacks) at the start of each day for 12 days.

**Tier 5 (25 days):** You gain all Cryovara Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may call a Glacier Fall from the sky at a point within 120 ft (30 ft radius). Creatures must make a DEX save (DC 16) or take 10d10 Cold damage and be buried under ice (Restrained). Those who succeed take half damage. You must, by the end of the pact, survive a situation where you were severely disadvantaged (environmental hazard, numerical disadvantage, etc.). Failure results in permanent half Vitality maximum and Greater Weakness to Fire.

## Indravajra
*Lightning*

**Tier 1 (1 day):** You gain Resistance to Lightning damage. Once per round, when a creature within 30 ft takes damage, you may deal 1d10 Lightning damage to it (no action required). You must, once per day, assert your dominance in a social situation (interrupt someone, issue a command, take control of a conversation). Failure results in you being unable to speak above a whisper for 1 day.

**Tier 2 (3 days):** Once per short rest, as a bonus action, you may call down a lightning strike on a point within 60 ft (10 ft radius). Creatures must make a DEX save (DC 13) or take 4d10 Lightning damage and be Stunned until the end of their next turn. You must, once per day, win a contest of strength, will, or skill against someone. Failure results in you being Stunned for 1 turn at the start of each combat for 3 days.

**Tier 3 (5 days):** Creatures that hit you with a melee attack take 3d10 Lightning damage and must make a CON save (DC 14) or be Stunned until the end of their next turn. You must, once per day, publicly challenge someone who disrespects you. Failure results in you being unable to take Reactions for 5 days.

**Tier 4 (10 days):** Once per long rest, you may take to the air for 1 minute. You gain a fly speed equal to your walking speed, your ranged attacks deal +2d10 Lightning damage, and you cannot be targeted by melee attacks unless the attacker can also fly. You must, by the end of the pact, make at least 3 creatures publicly submit to your authority or fear you. Failure results in you being Grounded (cannot fly or levitate) for 10 days.

**Tier 5 (22 days):** You gain all Indravajra Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you create a 100 ft radius storm overhead for 3 turns. At the start of each turn, lightning strikes all enemies of your choice in the area. Each must make a DEX save (DC 16) or take 6d10 Lightning damage and be Stunned for 1 turn. You must, by the end of the pact, defeat a powerful opponent in single combat or public contest. Failure results in permanent disadvantage on all CHA checks and Greater Weakness to Lightning.

## Seraphael
*Hallowed*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Hallowed damage. As a bonus action, you may cleanse yourself or an adjacent ally of one condition (Poisoned, Cursed, Withered, or Blinded). You may do this once per short rest. You must, once per day, help someone who cannot repay you. Failure results in you being Poisoned for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** Once per short rest, as an action, you may fire a 120 ft line of purifying light (10 ft wide). Creatures of the Evil Alignment take 4d10 Hallowed damage. Creatures with Withered, Corrupted, or Cursed take double damage. You must, once per day, refuse to let corruption go unchallenged (call out a lie, stop a theft, report a crime). Failure results in you gaining the Cursed condition whenever you enter combat for 4 days.

**Tier 3 (7 days):** You and allies within 30 ft are immune to Poison, Blight, and Cursed damage. Creatures of Evil alignment within this aura take 2d10 Hallowed damage at the start of their turns. You must, by the end of the pact, purify something (cleanse a cursed object, heal a diseased person, remove a corrupt influence). Failure results in you being unable to enter sacred or consecrated ground for 7 days.

**Tier 4 (12 days):** Once per long rest, you may create a 60 ft radius Sanctuary for 1 minute. Creatures of your choice cannot enter. Evil creatures that attempt to enter take 6d10 Hallowed damage. Allies inside heal 1d10 Vitality at the start of their turns. You must, by the end of the pact, protect someone who could not protect themselves for at least 3 days. Failure results in all your healing being halved for 12 days.

**Tier 5 (25 days):** You gain all Seraphael Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per turn as an Action you may purge evil. Choose a targeted creature of the Evil alignment, they take 100 Hallowed damage. There is no save. You must, by the end of the pact, convert at least one corrupted creature or location back to purity. Failure results in permanent inability to benefit from healing magic.

## Pelagion
*Torrent*

**Tier 1 (1 day):** You gain Resistance to Torrent damage. You may make a melee attack as a bonus action once per turn. You must, today, attack first in a conflict (be the one to strike the first blow). Failure results in you being Slowed for 1 day.

**Tier 2 (3 days):** When you deal damage to a creature below half Vitality, you deal +2d10 Torrent damage. You must, once per day, pursue a fleeing enemy or refuse to let a wounded opponent escape. Failure results in you being unable to deal extra damage of any kind for 3 days.

**Tier 3 (5 days):** Your melee reach increases by 10 ft. When you hit a creature, you may attempt to grapple it as a free action (no action required). You must, once per day, hunt and kill something (a monster, a challenging animal, or a hostile NPC). Failure results in your reach being reduced by 5 ft (minimum 5 ft) for 5 days.

**Tier 4 (10 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you make 3 melee attacks against a single target. Each hit deals 4d10 Torrent damage. If all 3 hit the same target, it takes an additional 6d10 Torrent damage. You must, by the end of the pact, kill at least 5 hostile creatures without showing mercy (no accepting surrender). Failure results in you being unable to take the Attack action for the first round of each combat for 10 days.

**Tier 5 (20 days):** You gain all Pelagion Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, you may enter a Feeding Frenzy for 1 minute. You gain Haste (double speed, +2 AC, one additional attack per action) and all your attacks heal you for 25% of damage dealt. You must, by the end of the pact, lead or participate in a raid, invasion, or major assault. Failure results in permanent half damage against all creatures.

## Keraunos
*Thunder*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Thunder and Lightning damage. Once per short rest, as a bonus action, you may speak with a voice that carries. All creatures within 60 ft that can hear you must make a WIS save (DC 12) or be Frightened of you until the end of your next turn. You must, once per day, give a command to someone and expect it to be followed. Failure results in you being unable to speak for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** Once per short rest, as an action, you call lightning on a point within 120 ft (20 ft radius). Creatures must make a DEX save (DC 14) or take 4d10 Lightning damage and be Deafened for 2 turns. Those who succeed take half damage. You must, once per day, demonstrate your power in a visible way (break something, use a loud spell, cause a scene). Failure results in Deafened at the start of each combat for 4 days.

**Tier 3 (7 days):** You control the water within 60 ft. As a bonus action, you may part shallow water, create a 20 ft wall of water, or make a whirlpool that Restrains one creature (STR save to escape). You must, by the end of the pact, make at least 3 creatures submit to your will through fear. Failure results in you being unable to swim and treating all water as difficult terrain for 7 days.

**Tier 4 (12 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you create a 100 ft radius storm for 3 turns. At the start of each turn, enemies in the area take 4d10 Thunder damage and 4d10 Lightning damage. You must, by the end of the pact, cause a visible change in the weather or environment through your actions. Failure results in you taking 2d10 Thunder damage at the start of each of your turns in combat for 12 days.

**Tier 5 (25 days):** You gain all Keraunos Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may call a Tidal Wave that surges forward 200 ft (60 ft wide, 30 ft tall). Creatures must make a STR save (DC 16) or take 10d10 Bludgeoning damage and be swept to the wave's endpoint. You must, by the end of the pact, conquer or control a coastal area or body of water. Failure results in permanent Greater Weakness to Thunder and Lightning.

## Hephaestion
*Metal*

**Tier 1 (3 days):** You gain Resistance to Metal damage and +2 AC. As a bonus action, you may repair a metal object you touch, restoring up to 20 HP of damage to it. You must, once per day, create or repair something with your hands (even a simple tool). Failure results in your AC being reduced by 2 for 3 days.

**Tier 2 (5 days):** Your melee attacks ignore 5 points of damage reduction or armor. Once per turn when you hit, you may reduce the target's AC by 1 until the end of combat (stacks up to -5). You must, once per day, inspect something closely for flaws and report them honestly. Failure results in your attacks dealing half damage for 5 days.

**Tier 3 (9 days):** You gain DR 10 against all physical damage. Your body is treated as one size category larger for the purpose of resisting grapple and forced movement. You must, by the end of the pact, forge or craft a high-quality item (GM decides quality). Failure results in your DR being removed and you taking double damage from physical attacks for 9 days.

**Tier 4 (14 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you create a divine metal cage around a target within 60 ft. The cage has 500 HP and AC 30. The target is trapped until the cage is destroyed or you release it. You must, by the end of the pact, teach someone a craft or skill that will outlast you. Failure results in you being trapped (Restrained) at the start of each combat for 14 days until you take 50 damage to break free.

**Tier 5 (30 days):** You gain all Hephaestion Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may reshape the terrain within 120 ft. Raise walls, open ditches, or smooth ground. Creatures caught in the reshaping take 8d10 Metal damage. You must, by the end of the pact, create a permanent structure that will survive for at least 10 years. Failure results in permanent -5 AC and all your equipment becoming brittle (breaks on a natural 1 attack roll).

## Aeolus
*Storm*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Storm/Wind damage. Your speed increases by 10 ft. You cannot be grappled or restrained by physical means that rely on contact (webs, nets, etc. still work). You must, once per day, spend at least 10 minutes in a high place or open area feeling the wind. Failure results in your speed being reduced by 10 ft for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** Once per short rest, as an action, you unleash a 60 ft cone of wind. Creatures must make a STR save (DC 13) or be pushed 60 ft away from you and take 3d10 Wind damage. Creatures pushed into obstacles take an additional 2d10 Bludgeoning damage. You must, once per day, travel to a new place you have never been before. Failure results in you being unable to move more than 30 ft from your starting position each day for 4 days.

**Tier 3 (7 days):** You can feel every air current within 300 ft. You know the exact position of all creatures in that range that are not magically hidden. You cannot be surprised. You must, by the end of the pact, free something or someone that was confined or imprisoned. Failure results in you being Blinded to hidden creatures and always surprised for 7 days.

**Tier 4 (12 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you create a 40 ft radius Cyclone at a point within 120 ft for 3 turns. Creatures inside must make a STR save (DC 15) each turn or be lifted 40 ft into the air and take 4d10 Slashing damage. You must, once per day, refuse an offer of permanent residence, binding contract, or long-term commitment. Failure results in you being unable to fly or levitate and your jump height being halved for 12 days.

**Tier 5 (25 days):** You gain all Aeolus Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may release a 200 ft cone of wind that transports non-allied creatures to a random location within 1 mile (no save for creatures smaller than Large; Large+ get a STR save). You must, by the end of the pact, travel to at least 3 different settlements or regions and leave a mark on each. Failure results in permanent Grounded condition and half speed.

## Rimeflame
*Frostfire*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to both Cold and Fire damage. Once per turn when you deal Cold or Fire damage, you may treat the target's resistance as one tier lower (if immune, treat as resistant). You must, once per day, expose yourself to extreme temperature (very hot or very cold). Failure results in you gaining either On Fire (2 stacks) or Frozen (2 stacks) each day for 2 days (your choice each day).

**Tier 2 (4 days):** When you deal Cold damage to a creature that is On Fire, you deal an additional 3d10 Frostfire damage and the target is Slowed until the end of its next turn. You must, once per day, switch between two extreme activities (hot to cold, fast to slow, loud to quiet). Failure results in your damage being halved against creatures that have any condition for 4 days.

**Tier 3 (7 days):** Once per short rest, as an action, you fire a 60 ft line of frostfire (15 ft wide). Creatures must make a CON save (DC 14) or take 6d10 Frostfire damage and gain both Frozen (2 stacks) and On Fire (2 stacks). Those who succeed take half damage. You must, once per day, endure something that tests your endurance for 10 minutes (run in place, hold a heavy object, hold your breath). Failure results in you starting each combat with both Frozen (2 stacks) and On Fire (2 stacks) for 7 days.

**Tier 4 (10 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you create a 40 ft radius Thermal Shockwave. All creatures must make a CON save (DC 15) or take 8d10 Frostfire damage. Their armor and weapons are degraded (-1 to AC or damage until repaired). You must, by the end of the pact, survive in an extreme environment for at least 24 consecutive hours. Failure results in permanent -2 AC until you complete a quest for Rimeflame.

**Tier 5 (22 days):** You gain all Rimeflame Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per turn when you deal Cold damage to a creature that is On Fire, it takes an additional 6d10 Frostfire damage and is Stunned for 1 turn. You must, by the end of the pact, convert at least 3 creatures to a lifestyle of endurance and thrill-seeking. Failure results in permanent Greater Weakness to both Cold and Fire.

## Cryolith
*Glacial*

**Tier 1 (3 days):** You gain Resistance to Glacial/Cold damage. You gain 20 temporary armor at the start of each day. You must, once per day, seal or lock something away (a container, a door, a memory). Failure results in your armor being 0 for 3 days.

**Tier 2 (5 days):** Creatures within 20 ft of you have their speed reduced by 10 ft. You are immune to this effect and move at full speed on ice or frozen ground. You must, once per day, refuse to take a risk (choose the safe option when a risky one is available). Failure results in your speed being halved and you being unable to take the Dash action for 5 days.

**Tier 3 (9 days):** Once per short rest, as an action, you fire a 60 ft cone of glacial shards. Creatures must make a DEX save (DC 14) or take 6d10 Glacial damage and gain Frozen (4 stacks). You must, by the end of the pact, successfully contain a threat (lock up a monster, seal a leak, imprison a criminal). Failure results in Frozen (5 stacks) at the start of each combat that cannot be reduced for 9 days.

**Tier 4 (14 days):** You gain 100 additional armor that regenerates at the start of each turn. While you have any armor, you cannot be moved by any force. You must, by the end of the pact, maintain a secret or keep a location hidden for the entire duration. Failure results in you being unable to gain armor from any source for 14 days.

**Tier 5 (28 days):** You gain all Cryolith Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may encase one creature within 60 ft in a block of ice. The ice has 800 HP and AC 30. The creature is Incapacitated until the ice is destroyed. You must, by the end of the pact, permanently seal away a significant threat (GM decides). Failure results in permanent Frozen condition (cannot be removed) until you serve Cryolith for 1 year.

## Gelidra
*Gel*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Gel damage. You can squeeze through openings as small as 6 inches wide (takes 1 extra movement per 5 ft). You must, once per day, bind or tie something securely (a rope, a package, a prisoner). Failure results in you being Restrained for 1 hour each day for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** Once per short rest, as an action, you splash a 30 ft cone of sticky gel. Creatures must make a DEX save (DC 13) or be Restrained for 2 turns. Restrained creatures take 3d10 Gel damage at the start of their turns. You must, once per day, successfully capture or restrain a creature (any size). Failure results in you being Restrained at the start of each combat until you take damage for 4 days.

**Tier 3 (7 days):** Creatures that hit you with a melee attack take 3d10 Gel damage and have their weapon or armor degraded (-1 to damage or AC until repaired). You take half damage from bludgeoning and slashing attacks. You must, by the end of the pact, capture a creature alive instead of killing it. Failure results in you taking double damage from bludgeoning and slashing for 7 days.

**Tier 4 (12 days):** As an action, you may collapse into a pool of gel and reform at any point within 60 ft. When you reform, creatures adjacent to you must make a DEX save (DC 14) or be Restrained. You may do this once per short rest. You must, once per day, use nonlethal means to subdue a threat. Failure results in you being unable to move more than 15 ft from your current position for 12 days.

**Tier 5 (24 days):** You gain all Gelidra Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may create a 40 ft radius pool of adhesive gel for 1 minute. Creatures inside are Slowed and must make a STR save (DC 15) each turn or be Restrained. Restrained creatures take 5d10 Gel damage at the start of their turns. You must, by the end of the pact, bind or capture at least 20 creatures. Failure results in permanent half movement speed and you cannot use teleportation effects.

## Mnemosyne
*Psychic*

**Tier 1 (3 days):** You gain Resistance to Psychic damage. Once per turn, when you damage a creature you have seen use an ability before, you may deal +1d10 Psychic damage and learn one piece of information about it (its current HP, a resistance, a condition immunity). You must, once per day, write down something you learned that day (keep a journal or record). Failure results in you forgetting the last 24 hours (amnesia for that period) for 3 days.

**Tier 2 (5 days):** As a bonus action, you may touch a creature or object and learn its recent history (last 24 hours). You may do this once per short rest. You must, once per day, recite from memory at least 3 facts about someone you spoke to. Failure results in you being unable to form new memories for 5 days (you function but remember nothing new).

**Tier 3 (9 days):** You have advantage on attacks against creatures you have fought before. You cannot be tricked by the same illusion or deception twice. You must, by the end of the pact, learn the names of at least 10 new people and remember them. Failure results in you forgetting the names of all allies and being unable to identify friend from foe for 9 days.

**Tier 4 (14 days):** Once per long rest, as an action, you fire a 120 ft line of psychic memory (10 ft wide). Creatures must make a WIS save (DC 15) or take 6d10 Psychic damage and forget the last 10 minutes (lose memory of the current combat). You must, by the end of the pact, uncover a secret that someone has kept for at least 1 year. Failure results in you losing all memory of the past month and having to relearn it.

**Tier 5 (28 days):** You gain all Mnemosyne Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may flood a 100 ft radius with ancestral memories. All non-allied creatures must make a WIS save (DC 16) or be overwhelmed, becoming Stunned for 1d4 turns. You must, by the end of the pact, preserve an important piece of knowledge or history that would otherwise be lost. Failure results in permanent memory loss — you lose all knowledge of one skill or language (your choice).

## Hemalux
*Bloodfire*

**Tier 1 (2 days):** You gain Resistance to Bloodfire damage. When you deal damage to a creature, you heal for 1d10 Vitality. This healing cannot exceed the damage dealt. You must, once per day, swear an oath to protect someone or something. Failure results in you being unable to regain Vitality for 2 days.

**Tier 2 (4 days):** Once per short rest, as a bonus action, you may swear an Oath of Protection on one ally within 30 ft. For 1 minute, while you are within 30 ft of that ally, any damage that would hit them instead hits you. You gain +2 AC while protecting them. You must, once per day, fulfill your sworn oath in a tangible way. Failure results in you being unable to protect anyone (all damage protection abilities fail) for 4 days.

**Tier 3 (8 days):** When you are below half Vitality, your melee attacks deal double damage. When below quarter Vitality, they deal triple damage and you may make one additional attack per turn. You must, once per day, put yourself in harm's way to protect someone else. Failure results in your damage being halved while below half Vitality for 8 days.

**Tier 4 (14 days):** Once per round, when an allied creature within 30 ft would take damage, you may intercept that damage, taking it yourself instead. You gain +3 AC for 1 turn after doing so. You must, by the end of the pact, successfully protect someone from a genuine threat (not a staged one). Failure results in you being unable to take damage for allies (all interception abilities fail) for 14 days.

**Tier 5 (28 days):** You gain all Hemalux Tier 1–4 benefits while this pact is active. In addition, once per day, as an action, you may sacrifice half your current Vitality to grant all allied creatures within 120 ft immunity to all damage and conditions for 2 turns. You must, by the end of the pact, die for a cause or come within 1 HP of death while protecting someone. Failure results in permanent cowardice — you cannot willingly take damage for another creature ever again.



========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/6.0 Magic/6.1 Spells.md
========================================================================

Spells are learned as base forms, with more powerful versions accessed through upcasting. When a spell is learned, only its base form is known. As the caster advances, they may expend additional mana to cast that same spell at higher tiers, transforming it into more powerful versions with expanded effects. These higher forms are not separate spells and do not need to be learned individually.

Upcasting increases a spell’s power based on its design. This may include increases to damage, range, area, duration, number of targets, or additional effects such as conditions or environmental impact. Each spell defines how it scales, and not all spells scale in the same way. Most spells use a multiplicative mana increase (commonly x2 per tier), though more powerful jumps may require higher scaling such as x3 or greater (and some are lesser). The final listed form of a spell is not a limit. Once a caster reaches that form, the spell may continue to be upcast further according to its scaling rules, allowing for extreme late-game power at significant mana cost.

Spells are governed by Affinity and Mana. The Affinity tied to the spell determines its effectiveness, while Mana determines how often and how strongly it can be cast. More specialized or difficult-to-obtain affinities produce significantly stronger spells, but require greater investment to access and scale.

Some spells require Concentration, remaining active only while the caster maintains focus. Casting another concentration spell ends the previous one immediately. If the caster takes damage to Vitality or Health, they must succeed a concentration check or lose the spell. When a spell applies a condition without specifying its duration, it uses the standard condition system, but the removal DC is replaced with the spell’s save DC.
#### Quick Reference
- **Spell Attack Modifier** = Generic Affinity + Affinity Mod + Base Magic Accuracy
	- Your Affinity mod is based on your relevant affinity, you can calculate it as follows (rounded up): **(Affinity - 2) / 2**
	- Examples:
		- -5 Affinity = -3.5 (-3) Affinity Mod
		- -2 Affinity = -2 Affinity Mod
		- 0 Affinity = -1 Affinity Mod
		- 1 Affinity = -0.5 (0) Affinity Mod
		- 2 Affinity = 0 Affinity Mod
		- 3 Affinity = +0.5 (+1) Affinity Mod
		- 5 Affinity = +1.5 (+2) Affinity Mod
		- 10 Affinity = +4 Affinity Mod
- **Spell Attack Damage** = Specified Damage + Base Magic Damage
- **Spell Save DC** = 10 + Generic Affinity + Relevant Affinity
	- **Condition Saves:** Conditions imposed from spells use your Spell Save DC instead of their written DC in [[2.4 Conditions]] unless otherwise specified. 
- **Concentration Check** = 1d20 vs DC 12 on taking damage
- **Upcasting** = Defined per spell, typically x2 mana per tier

> For damage type references see [[2.2 Damage Types]]. For condition references see [[2.4 Conditions]].

> **How upcasting works in practice**
>
> When you learn Firebolt, you learn its base form: 5 mana, 1d8 fire damage. You don't need to find or learn Fireball separately. You just cast Firebolt with more mana.
>
> **Here's how Firebolt scales:**
> - Mana cost: 5 (Firebolt) → 8 (Fireball) → 13 (Fireburst) → 22 (Inferno Ring) → 42 (Cataclysm Pyre)
> - Each tier multiplies the mana roughly by 1.5-2x.
> - Damage goes from 1d8 → 2d6 (AoE) → 4d6 + Burned → 5d6 + persistent AoE → 8d6 + ground fire.
>
> **The key insight:** You never outgrow your spells. That level 1 Firebolt you picked at character creation is still viable at level 20, you're just casting it at Cataclysm Pyre tier by then. This means every spell you learn stays relevant forever.
>
> **Upcasting beyond the listed forms:** The final form (Cataclysm Pyre for Fire) isn't the end. Its upcast says "x2, +3d6 blast damage." If you have the mana, you can keep scaling. A level 30 Fire mage might cast it at 84 mana for 11d6+ground fire. The only limit is your mana pool and affinity.

| Power Word: Pain   |   30 | 60                  | Shatter   | Choose a creature with 100 HP or fewer. It is wracked with excruciating pain. It is Stunned for 1 turn and its max HP is reduced by 20 until a Long Rest. This effect cannot kill the creature, if it's health would be reduced below 5 it is reduced to 5 instead.                                                                                                                         | x2, HP reduction +20                                                                         | >16 Shatter Affinity         |
## Healing Spells
All healing spells are known at all times by any creature with any levels in The Healer. Other prerequisites must still be met. These spells cannot be learned without having at least 1 level in The Healer. Casting a healing spell on an Undead creature will instead deal damage equal to the healing amount. If you use Hallowed instead of Generic affinity the damage is tripled.

========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/6.0 Magic/6.2 Runes.md
========================================================================

All runes can be purchased as single use items from stores, runes are drawn on Runic Material, explained lower down. They can then be used for enchanting and spells. Runes cannot be activated without a level in Runemaster path. 
# Runic Inscription (Enchanting)
Runes are ancient symbols etched into items to tap into the fundamental forces of the world.
- Xeran Runes: Used for Permanent Enchantments. Once applied, the item is permanently changed.
- Vindroth Runes: Used for complex Spellcasting. Will be explained lower down.
### Runic Logic & Constraints
- A single Xeran rune can be etched onto an item with no penalty. However, if you wish to place two or more effect runes on a single object, they must be bound within a Runic Circle.
- Enchanted items cannot be modified. You must decide the full runic array before the first inscription.
- A single runic circle can support a maximum of 10 Effect Runes.
- The Carbon Ratio: In any circle, the volatile energies must be balanced by Carbon Runes. The formula is:
	- *Number of Carbon Runes = Number of Effect Runes - 1*
## Inscription Requirements & Circle Scaling
Creating a runic circle requires a base Mana expenditure plus the cost of the individual runes. The mana Surcharge cost is added to EVERY rune in the circle as you roll them (carbon runes are exempt from this charge). 

| **Effect Runes** | **Carbon Runes** | **Total Circle Size** | **Mana Surcharge** | **Level Req.** |
| ---------------- | ---------------- | --------------------- | ------------------ | -------------- |
| **1**            | 0                | 1                     | +5                 | 1              |
| **2**            | 1                | 3                     | +10                | 1              |
| **3**            | 2                | 5                     | +20                | 3              |
| **4**            | 3                | 7                     | +50                | 6              |
| **5**            | 4                | 9                     | +100               | 12             |
| **6**            | 5                | 11                    | +200               | 21             |
| **7**            | 6                | 13                    | +300               | 30             |
| **8**            | 7                | 15                    | +400               | 40             |
| **9**            | 8                | 17                    | +500               | 55             |
| **10**           | 9                | 19                    | +650               | 70             |
## Xeran Rune Effects Table
Effects stack. If a weapon has two Fire runes, it deals +2d6 Fire damage and so on.
The DC mod of a rune is added to the inscription check. If you are inscribing a rune onto a weapon or armor which is made from a special material (see [[2.6 Materials]]) it may have an enchantability score. That score is subtracted from the DC mod (For example a Gold item has an enchantability of 12 so if you were to enchant a Golden Sword with Unbreaking the DC mod goes from 8 to -4, this would mean when you make the check the DC is 4 lower than a base roll with no mod).

| **Rune**           | **Effect (Weapon)**                                                                                                                                                 | **Effect (Armor)**                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Mana Cost** | DC Mod | Affinity | Cost       |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------- |
| Carbon             | None (Stabilizer)                                                                                                                                                   | None (Stabilizer)                                                                                                                                                                                       | 0             | 0      | Generic  | 1 Gold     |

| Greater Energy     | +2 Accuracy Bonus                                                                                                                                                   | +2 Armor Class (AC)                                                                                                                                                                                     | 10            | 6      | Generic  | 4 Gold     |
| Resist             | None                                                                                                                                                                | Grants lesser resistance to an element it is paired with (two resist plus one element will grant regular Resistance, three will grant Greater Resistance, each additional beyond that will grant +2 AC) | 50            | 9      | Generic  | 1 Nerite   |

## The Inscription Ritual
Inscribing a runic array is a delicate process of channeling mana through physical matter. To successfully enchant an item, a crafter must perform a sequence of Inscription Checks, one for every rune in the array (including Carbon runes).

1. The Inscription Check
	- For each rune, roll a d20 and add your Relevant Affinity Modifier.
	- The Base DC is 12 and it goes up depending on the DC Mod (see above table).
		- **Example**: Inscribing a Fire Rune has a total DC of 16.
	- When you make the check for the rune you must spend its mana cost. The cost is calculated by the cost of the rune and the size of the circle (the Mana Surcharge).
		- **Example**: The mana cost for a Fire rune in a 3 rune circle would be 4 (from the rune itself) + 20 (for the mana surcharge). This mana will be spent for EACH rune in the circle after a successful check is made. You do not need to spend any mana on the carbon runes. 

2. The Ritual Sequence
	- If you are creating a Runic Circle, you must roll for every rune in the circle, pick the first rune to inscribe (it doesn't matter which one) and then go around the circle at your leisure. Mana is spent as you go. You must outline the entire circle and put a blank space for each rune before you begin the inscription. 
	- If you pass the roll, the rune is successfully etched. Move to the next rune in the array. There is no time limit in between runic checks, you may wait as long as you need before attempting to inscribe the next rune. Until all runes in the circle are inscribed the runes will do nothing. 
	- **Critical Success (Natural 20**): The rune is etched perfectly; you gain Advantage on the next roll in the sequence, **no mana is spent on this rune**.

3. Failed Inscriptions & Unstable Mana
	- If a single roll in the sequence fails the ritual fails and the item becomes saturated with unstable mana.
	- The item cannot be enchanted further, and the current runes provide no bonuses and are destroyed.
	- To clear the Saturated state you must spend some mana to fix the item, the mana cost will change based on how much mana you spent so far using the below formula. Once fixed, the item is restored to its original, non-magical state and can be enchanted again from scratch, ALL runes are destroyed in this process.
		- **Desaturation formula:** (Mana Spent / 3) x (Number of Runes Inscribed + 5)
		- **Example:** Say you successfully inscribed 2 of 3 fire runes in a runic circle that was meant to be 3 fire runes. 
			- The cost of one Fire rune inscription would be 4 (from the rune itself) + 20 (for the mana surcharge) so that's 24 mana per rune meaning we spent 48 mana. 
			- Divide the mana we spent by 3 (always round down if applicable), 48 / 3 = 16. 
			- Now multiply the mana cost by the number of runes inscribed plus 5 (2 runes + 5 = 7). This would be 16 x 7 = 112.
			- So in order to desaturate the item/inscription you would need to spend 112 mana. 
		- **Note:** Carbon runes have no mana cost so they are not included in this, if you failed and have only done checks on Carbon runes and nothing else it will cost nothing to desaturate it. 

> **The ritual step by step, with a full walkthrough**
>
> Let's say you want to inscribe a 3-Fire-rune circle on a steel sword. You have +5 Fire Affinity and 80 mana to work with.
>
> **Step 1: Outline the circle:** You draw a circle big enough for 5 runes (3 Fire + 2 Carbon). Before you start inscribing, the runes do nothing.
>
> **Step 2: First rune:** You pick a spot and attempt the first Fire rune. The DC is 12 + 4 (DC Mod) = 16. You roll a 13 + 5 = 18. Success! You spend 2 (rune) + 20 (surcharge) = 22 mana. One down.
>
> **Step 3: Second rune:** Same DC. You roll a 9 + 5 = 14. Failure. The entire circle is ruined. You've spent 22 mana so far.
>
> **Step 4: Desaturation:** To clear the sword, you need to pay (22 / 3) × (1 + 5) = 7 × 6 = 42 mana. The sword is now clean but you're down 64 total mana and have nothing to show for it.
>
> **The smart approach:** If you had inscribed both Carbon runes first (free, no risk of losing mana since they cost 0), then attempted the three Fire runes, you'd have a buffer. And if you roll a natural 20 on any rune, that rune costs 0 mana and you get Advantage on the next roll.
>
> **Pro tip:** Materials with high Enchantability (like Gold, +12) turn DC 16 checks into DC 4 checks. A +5 Fire mage with a gold sword literally cannot fail a Fire rune inscription (except by rolling a natural 1 which always fails). Always factor your material's enchantability into your plans, but remember that if the material isn't listed in [[2.6 Materials]] it has no enchantability modifier. 
## Making New Elements
When you combine runes of different elements, you can create **new damage types** and their associated effects. Use the **[[2.1 Affinities]]** list to see which elements combine into which results. Rune combinations are influenced by the user’s intent, meaning intermediate pairings don’t have to occur if they aren’t the intended outcome. For example, Obsidian is made from Ice/Cold + Blight. Since there are no direct Ice/Cold or Blight runes, you instead build them from their components: (Water + Air) to represent Ice/Cold, and (Necrotic + Earth) to represent Blight, so the full set is Water + Air + Necrotic + Earth. In this case, Air + Necrotic does not automatically form Toxin/Poison, because the declared intent is to form Ice/Cold + Blight, which then combines into Obsidian. These combined elements can be used the same ways as normal elemental runes.

Greater Elemental runes and regular elemental runes will not combine together.
# Vindroth Runes
Vindroth runes are written as sentence structures (often called _paragraphs_ or spells), allowing rune magic to function like a programmed trigger system, when a condition is met, an effect occurs. Vindroth runes are not arranged in circles. Instead, they are inscribed as ordered “words” that form a readable sequence (ex: If … Than … Else …).
### Inscription Rules
- Each Vindroth rune occupies 1 square inch of space.
- All runes in a paragraph must be inscribed into one continuous piece of material.
- If the material has a complete break that separates words, the paragraph loses the separated words. This may weaken, change, or fully disable the effect.
### Vindroth Structure Limits
A Vindroth paragraph is limited by your Level.
- You may include 1 “If … Than” block per your Level/2.
- Every 2 Levels also increases how many logic-connectors you may include (Else / And / Or / Not).
### Using Xeran as Effect Payloads
Vindroth can call Xeran runes as effect payloads (usually to define damage type / element / output).
Example structure:
- If object (human) entered size (5) shape (sphere) Than Power (Xeran Fire)
To increase output, you may repeat effect runes:
- … Than Power (Xeran Fire) And Power (Xeran Fire)
With each And Power adding another damage as well as consuming 1 Structure Limit
## Vindroth Conditionals
> If a Vindroth spell loops it can only activate once per turn (6 seconds) unless it is paired with a Speed Up rune.

| Rune          | Effect                                                                                                       | Cost     |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------- |
| if            | Begins a conditional check.                                                                                  | 5 Silver |
| than          | Executes the next statement(s) if the condition is true.                                                     | 5 Silver |
| else          | Executes the next statement(s) if the previous condition was false (may be followed by **If**).              | 5 Silver |
| and           | Combines conditions/effects; both must apply (when used with **If**) or both occur (when used with effects). | 5 Silver |
| or            | Combines conditions; either condition being true is sufficient.                                              | 5 Silver |
| not           | Negates a condition (true only if the condition is not met).                                                 | 5 Silver |
| at            | Specifies the point of origin / location where the effect occurs.                                            | 1 Silver |
| while         | Keeps the effect active as long as the condition remains true.                                               | 1 Silver |
| size          | Defines area size; maximum area is 5 × (Level / 3) ft, minimum of 5 ft.                                      | 1 Silver |
| shape         | Defines area shape (sphere, cube, spear (directional attack)).                                               | 1 Silver |
| object        | Defines what is being checked or targeted (leave blank to mean “any”).                                       | 1 Silver |
| within        | True if the object is within the specified range/area.                                                       | 1 Silver |
| entered       | True if the object enters the specified range/area.                                                          | 1 Silver |
| value         | Stores a named value (variable).                                                                             | 1 Silver |
| equals        | True if two values are equal.                                                                                | 1 Silver |
| greater       | True if the first value is greater than the second.                                                          | 1 Silver |
| lesser        | True if the first value is less than the second.                                                             | 1 Silver |
| has           | True if the object has a specified property/flag/mark.                                                       | 1 Silver |
| lacks         | True if the object lacks a specified property/flag/mark.                                                     | 1 Silver |
| self          | True if the object is the caster.                                                                            | 1 Silver |

| moved         | True if the object moved.                                                                                    | 1 Silver |
| attacked      | True if the object attacked.                                                                                 | 1 Silver |
| damaged       | True if the object took damage.                                                                              | 1 Silver |
| healed        | True if the object was healed.                                                                               | 1 Silver |

| chance        | Roll 1d10 and return the number (usable with comparisons like greater/lesser/equals).                        | 1 Silver |

| delay         | Delays an effect in 6-second intervals, max 18 × Level.                                                      | 1 Silver |
## Vindroth Effects
> Any rune which applies a condition which can be stacked applies one stack of said condition, each application will stack it. If the effect cannot be stacked then the amount of time the condition lasts for will be refreshed. 

> If a speed up and speed down rune are used together the spell will happen once every two turns but happen twice each time it activates.

| Stun            | Applies the Stunned condition (Con save 8 + Generic Affinity).                                                                                                               | 20 Silver |
| Cancer          | Applies the Radiation condition (Con save 8 + Atomic Affinity).                                                                                                              | 20 Silver |
| Wither          | Applies the Withered condition (Con save 10 + Blight Affinity).                                                                                                              | 20 Silver |
| Demoralize      | Applies the Demoralized condition (Wis save 10 + Generic Affinity).                                                                                                          | 20 Silver |
| Curse           | Applies the Cursed condition (Wis save 10 + Generic Affinity).                                                                                                               | 20 Silver |
| Hex             | Applies the Hexed condition (Wis save 10 + Generic Affinity).                                                                                                                | 20 Silver |
| Slow            | Applies the Slowed condition (Wis save 10 + Generic Affinity).                                                                                                               | 20 Silver |
| Hasten          | Applies the Hasted condition (Wis save 10 + Generic Affinity).                                                                                                               | 20 Silver |
| Blessed         | Applies the Blessed condition (Wis save 10 + Generic Affinity).                                                                                                              | 20 Silver |
## Example Castings

### Simple Fire Trap (Entry Trigger)
**Structure**
```
If object (any) entered size (5) shape (sphere)
Than Power (Xeran Fire)
```
**What it Does**  
Deals 1d4 Fire damage to any creature that enters a 5 ft sphere around the inscription.

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 1
- Logic connectors: 0

**Minimum Level**
- **1**

**Notes**  
Baseline Vindroth usage. This is the canonical rune trap example.
### Healing Plate (Touch Trigger)
**Structure**
```
If object (self) touched
Than Heal
```
**What it Does**  
When the caster touches the inscribed surface, they heal 1d4 HP.

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 1
- Logic connectors: 0

**Minimum Level**
- **1**
### Directional Force Push Trap
**Structure**
```
If object (any) entered size (5) shape (sphere)
Than Push direction (north)
```
**What it Does**  
Any creature entering the area must make a Strength save or be pushed north.

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 1
- Logic connectors: 0

**Minimum Level**
- **1**
### Conditional Trap with Else Clause
**Structure**
```
If object (self) entered size (5) shape (sphere)
Than Heal (Vitality)
Else Power (Xeran Necrotic)
```
**What it Does**
- If the caster enters: heal 1d6 Vitality
- Otherwise: intruders take 1d4 Necrotic damage

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 1
- Else clauses: 1

**Minimum Level**
- **4**
### Mana-Scaling Kill Zone
**Structure**
```
While object (any) within size (10) shape (sphere)
Than Power (Xeran Fire)
And Power (Xeran Fire)
And Power (Xeran Fire)
```
**What it Does**  
Deals 3d4 Fire damage to all creatures within the area each turn until they leave area

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 1 (while)
- Logic connectors: 2 (extra Power via And)

**Minimum Level**
- **6**
### Damage with Save and Delay
**Structure**
```
If object (any) entered size (5) shape (sphere)
Than delay (6)
And Power (Xeran Fire)
And throw (Dex)
And throw (10)
```
**What it Does**  
After 6 seconds, deals 1d4 Fire damage to creatures in the area, Dex save (10) to resist.

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 1
- Logic connectors: 3 (delay, And Power, And throw)

**Minimum Level**
- **8**
### Mark-and-Execute Rune
**Structure**
```
If object (any) entered size (5) shape (sphere)
Than Mark (prey)

If object (any) has (prey)
And attacked
Than Power (Xeran Fire)
And Power (Xeran Fire)
```
**What it Does**
- First trigger: entering marks the target.
- Second trigger: if a marked target attacks, they take 2d4 Fire damage.

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 2
- Logic connectors: 2

**Minimum Level**
- **8**
### Smart Defense Engine
**Structure**
```
If object (any) entered size (20) shape (sphere)
And lacks (ally)
Than Mark (hostile)

If object (any) has (hostile)
And cast
Than Dispel

If object (any) has (hostile)
And attacked
Than Power (Xeran Force)
And Power (Xeran Force)
And throw (Str)
And throw (15)

Else Heal
```
**What it Does**
- Marks non-allies entering the area
- Dispels hostile spellcasting
- Punishes hostile attacks with 2d4 Force damage (Strength save)
- Heals allies when no hostile condition is met

**Structure Use**
- If/Than blocks: 3
- And connectors: 6
- Else: 1

**Minimum Level**
- 20
# Making Your Own Runes
In order to make your own runes you must first be at least a level 5 Runemaster. See [[3.3 Leveling Up]] for more.

Rather than buying runes from a store you can make them yourself. The result is a single-use Xeran or Vindroth rune identical to a store-bought one. It can be sold, traded, or used in the Inscription Ritual.
### Materials
You need a Blank Runic Medium and Affinity Ink. See [[2.6 Materials]] for pricing or make them yourself using the rules below.
##### Making Blank Runic Medium
1. Take any material with an Enchantability of 5 or greater (see [[2.6 Materials]]). You need about 2 oz.
2. Flatten the material so it has at least 1 square inch of space to draw on, the flattening process will depend on the material you chose but they are typically metal so they can usually be forged.
3. Imbue it with 25 mana and make a DC 12 Arcana check. If you succeed it is now a Blank Runic Medium. On failure the material is destroyed.
##### Making Affinity Ink
1. Take 1 oz of crushed obsidian, voidsand, or void slime mass.
2. Imbue it with 25 mana and make a DC 12 Arcana check. If you succeed it is now Affinity Ink. On failure the material is destroyed.
### Manufacturing The Rune
Once you have a Blank Runic Medium and Affinity Ink, follow these steps:

1. **Draw the Rune.** Use your Affinity Ink to draw the rune onto the Blank Runic Medium. It does not matter what you draw with (a feather, a stick, your finger, etc). To draw the rune you must know what it looks like. You know what it looks like if you have another copy of it you can see or if you have successfully inscribed or made it 10 or more times. Make a Sleight of Hand check (DC 15). If you have another rune next to you to copy and have inscribed or made it 10+ times you have advantage on this roll.

2. **Activate the Rune.** Spend the rune's Mana Cost (see Xeran Rune Effects Table; for Vindroth runes the cost is 10) and make an Arcana check (DC = 12 + the rune's DC Mod; for Vindroth runes the DC mod is 5). You may add your Runic DC Check bonus (from Rune Master Level 3 / 3.5) to this roll.

**Success:** You create a fully functional rune.
**Failure:** The materials and mana are consumed and destroyed. 



========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/7.0 Monsters (WIP)/7.1 Monsters.md
========================================================================

Monsters are just creatures which the player will interact with in the World of Eyum. Most are aggressive but not all. Monsters can take all actions that players can take, however they are not as intelligent as a player so they shouldn't take super intelligent actions. 

Each monster fulfills a specific combat role, which reflects how it fights and what function it serves in an encounter. These roles include:
- **Berserker** - Constant frenzy of attacks
- **Tanker** - Tries to take hits to allow other monsters to deal damage
- **Guerrilla** - Attacks hard then disengages and runs away and hides
- **Ranger** - Attack from a distance
- **Stealth** - Sneak up on the player and deal lots of damage then sneak away
- **Mixed** - Is capable of attacking from melee and ranged
- **Support** - Makes other monsters stronger or summons other monsters
- **Swarmer** - Attack in large groups and simply overwhelm players with numbers

A monster’s role influences how it positions itself, what targets it prioritizes, and how it applies pressure during combat.

Monsters are also defined by an affinity, which represents the primary type of energy or damage they embody. Lower-difficulty monsters tend to use more common and easily understood affinities such as Fire, Earth, Water, Air, Radiant, and Necrotic, while stronger monsters may exhibit rarer or more complex affinities. Psychic-affinity creatures are uncommon and appear less frequently.

A monster’s main affinity is applied to its attacks as normal and typically grants it Resistance to that same damage type. Weaknesses usually align naturally with the chosen affinity.

A list of the monster types is as follows:

- Elemental Monsters
	- [[7.3.1 Slimes]]
	- [[7.3.2 Golems]]
	- [[7.3.3 Dragons]]
- Regular Monsters
	- [[7.3.4 Chimera]]
	- [[7.3.6 Unique Monsters]]
- Other
- [[7.3.5 Monster Modifiers]]

> Elemental monsters are monsters which have a unique type for every affinity/element. 
### Difficulty Rating (DR)
A monster’s **Difficulty Rating (DR)** is an estimate of how many level 5 players are required to defeat a single instance of that monster.
- **DR 1** indicates a monster that a single level 5 player can reasonably defeat alone.
- **DR 2** indicates a monster that would require approximately two level 5 players.
- **DR 1/5** represents a much weaker creature, closer in strength to what a level 1 player could handle.
- Creatures weaker than this can be treated as **DR 0**.

DR is not exact. It is intended as a quick reference for encounter balancing rather than a strict calculation.
### Monster Levels
Monsters can have levels, which increase their overall power.

Each level provides the following:
- +1 Hit Die to Vitality
- +1 Hit Die to Health
- +1 Hit Die to Mana
- +1 Generic Damage Mod
- +1 to the monster’s main Affinity
- +1 AC
- +1 to all Stats (Str, Con, Dex (every 3 levels (3,6,9,etc) gain +1 to Wis, Int, and Char as well))

> Each time you get a Hit Die you can roll for it or just add the average number to the total. In the handbook the averages are always added together and shown as (#) next to the die. 
> The generic damage mod is applied to all damage the creature deals. Typically the Affinity of the creature is also added to the attacks so each level up adds around 2 damage to each attack. Note that the generic damage added is always the damage type of the affinity of the creature. 
## Example Monster
**Generic Fire Monster**
- Level 1
> The Level of the monster is 1, so this is its base form, in the next example we will look at the same monster at a higher level. In the handbook monsters are all written as level 1 and you must scale them up manually to your desired level. 
- DR: 1
- AC: 13
> One level five player should be enough to beat this, or hopefully five level one players. Obviously there are so many variables in a fight that this number becomes almost useless but it may give a rough idea of the strength of an enemy.
- Speed: 30
- Type: Berserker
> This is typically used as a guide on how to play the monster. So in combat you would have this monster constantly attacking with no regard to its own health, it would never retreat. 
- Affinity: Fire (+2)
> This functions similarly to player affinity with one exception, its added to all attacks not just magical ones. It's added to saving throw DC's as well. 
- Generic Damage Mod: +0
> Added to all damage dealt by this monster no matter what
- Resistance: Fire
> Always the same as the main Affinity
- Weakness: Water, Ice/Cold
> For weakness, use the opposing affinity. For composite affinities, determine the opposite of each component affinity, then combine those opposites into a resulting affinity; this combined affinity is the weakness. If multiple valid combinations exist, all resulting affinities are considered weaknesses. If no valid combination can be formed, each individual base opposite is treated as a weakness.
- Vitality: 2d10 (10)
> For every point in Con above 10 add +1 Vitality. Do not subtract any health below 10. 
- Health: 1d8 (4)
- Mana: 2d12 (12)
- Stats: STR: 10 | CON: 10 | DEX: 10 | WIS: 4 | INT: 4 | CHAR: 4 
> Unlike a player the Stats don't have much influence other than Con. 

**Passives**
- **Hot to the Touch (Thorns):** When hit by a melee or touch-range attack, the attacker takes 1d4 Affinity damage.
- **Flame Body (Affinity Body):** If this creature takes damage matching its Affinity during the round, it heals 1d4 + Affinity Vitality at the start of its next turn.
- **Creature of Mana:** If Mana falls below 1, the creature takes 1d4 true damage at the start of its turn.
- **Fast Action:** At levels 5 and above gain +1 Ap.
> Being able to heal makes this creature really strong, it almost ensures its Kamikaze attacks will be able to happen until it runs out of mana. 

**Actives**
- **Claw:** Make a melee attack against a creature in rage, the target takes 1d6 piercing damage.
> Remember that generic damage mod is added to all damage. 
- **Kamikaze:** Creature explodes dealing 1d8+Affinity damage to all creatures within range, DC 10+Affinity Con save to half damage. The creature takes 2 Affinity damage.
> Kamikaze deals a damage to the creature ensuring they heal some and constantly explode until they run out of mana.

This creature is very powerful but all you have to do to win is simply outlast it. 
## Level 5 Example
**Generic Fire Monster**
- Level 5
- DR: 1
- AC: 18
> Note that the DR did not raise, this is because the DR is based on the level 1 monster. 
- Speed: 30
- Type: Berserker
- Affinity: Fire (+7)
- Generic Damage Mod: +7
> So for most attacks you would add +14 damage
- Resistance: Fire
- Weakness: Water, Ice/Cold
- Vitality: 10d10 + 5 (55)
- Health: 5d8 (20)
- Mana: 10d12 (60)
- Stats: STR: 15 | CON: 15 | DEX: 15 | WIS: 5 | INT: 5 | CHAR: 5 

**Passives**
- **Hot to the Touch (Thorns):** When hit by a melee or touch-range attack, the attacker takes 1d4 Affinity damage.
- **Flame Body (Affinity Body):** If this creature takes damage matching its Affinity during the round, it heals 1d4 + Affinity Vitality at the start of its next turn.
- **Creature of Mana:** If Mana falls below 1, the creature takes 1d4 true damage at the start of its turn.
- **Fast Action:** At levels 5 and above gain +1 Ap.

**Actives**
- **Claw:** Make a melee attack against a creature in rage, the target takes 1d6 piercing damage.
- **Kamikaze:** Creature explodes dealing 1d8+Affinity damage to all creatures within range, DC 10+Affinity Con save to half damage. The creature takes 2 Affinity damage.

At higher levels, the same monster becomes significantly more dangerous due to increases in Generic Damage Mod and Affinity, both of which apply across all of its damage sources. This allows it to deal meaningful damage through both direct attacks and passive effects, while maintaining its role as an aggressive, high-pressure threat.

The following passives and actives are universal and can be found in any monsters stat blocks. Some monsters may have unique skills like Slime Regeneration and Golem Overcharge. Monster specific skills can be found in their individual stat blocks. 
## Passives
Passive abilities are always active unless otherwise stated. Names may vary, but identical mechanics should remain consistent. Alternate names should be written as Name (BaseName). 

> An example of this naming would be Hot to the Touch (Thorns).

========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/7.0 Monsters (WIP)/7.3 Monster Lists/7.3.1 Slimes.md
========================================================================

Slimes are elemental organisms composed of semi-liquid matter bound around a central core. Their bodies lack fixed anatomy, instead forming unstable, shifting masses that flow, stretch, and reform continuously. The outer body, referred to as the _mass_, varies in viscosity depending on the slime's elemental affinity. Some are thick and sluggish, others loose and fluid, but all maintain cohesion through an internal anchor.

At the center of most slimes is a _core_, a dense concentration of elemental matter that stabilizes the creature. This core is the only truly vital component of a slime. The surrounding mass acts as both protection and function, allowing movement, interaction, and defense, but it is not required for survival. A slime reduced to only its core remains alive and will gradually regenerate its lost mass over time.

Slimes do not possess traditional organs, bones, or nervous systems. Instead, their awareness and control originate from the core, which directs the surrounding material as if it were an extension of itself. Their movement is typically achieved through slow shifting, rolling, or pulling their mass forward, though less viscous variants may flow rapidly or spread across surfaces. They are able to sense all creatures and objects around them at a range of 120 ft but do not possess any way to hear, smell, or actually see them.

Slimes reproduce through merging. Under normal conditions, slimes will only successfully combine with others of the same elemental type, splitting afterward into new individuals (two slimes merge into four all at one quarter the size of the original). In rare cases, incompatible slimes can be forcibly combined. When this occurs, their cores destabilize and reform into a new configuration, producing a higher-order slime aligned with a more complex affinity. These higher slimes follow the same structural rules as their base forms but exhibit stronger properties and more volatile behavior. When a slime reproduces the four remaining slimes levels will be one quarter of the level of the total levels of the original slimes. Slimes cannot reproduce unless their total levels are at least four.

A slimes size will be determined by its Level. Unless otherwise stated all slimes are approximately 3 ft diameter spheres which squish into a half sphere on the ground. Each level will increase their diameter by 1 ft. A slime's affinity determines all other aspects of its composition. The texture, color, density, and behavior of its mass are direct expressions of its element. All slimes are immune to their own affinity and exhibit weakness to the natural opposing force. While most slimes conform to the core-and-mass structure, exceptions exist. Some variants deviate from this pattern, sacrificing stability for adaptability or regenerative capability.

Slime (mass) which is removed from the core is no longer controllable by the core. It can be used as a material and has the same properties of its affinity. All slimes when killed will drop some amount of their Mass which can be collected and sold and used in potions and such, the mass must be collected in a bottle of some kind. The price of each slimes mass varies based on type. Slime is collected in 32 oz jars and measured accordingly. You can collect up to 1d4 jars of slime per slime killed. The remaining mass will quickly dissipate after one hour.
## Stat Block Table for All Slimes
> For more information on how to level up a monster see [[7.1 Monsters]].

All slimes have the following passives
- **Slime Regeneration**: After the slime reaches 0 HP it does not die as usual, instead all of its mass spills away from the Core. The core must then be destroyed in order for it to actually be destroyed. All Core's have 10 Hp and no Vitality or AC. A slime counts as unconscious while it has no mass. Every hour after it has been "killed" it will regenerate 1d4 of health and a small amount of its mass. The Slime will remain unconscious until it has regained all of its Hp, it will then instantly regain consciousness and all vitality and mana. The Core cannot be regenerated if it takes damage. 
- **Affinity Body**: If this creature takes damage matching its Affinity during the round, it heals 1d4 + Affinity Vitality at the start of its next turn.
- **Scratch Resistance**: Take -(1 + Affinity) damage from slashing/piercing damage.

| Name       | DR  | AC  | Speed | Type       | Affinity | Gen Dmg Mod | Resistance | Weakness                                 | Vitality    | Health     | Mana       | Stats (STR/CON/DEX/WIS/INT/CHA) | Passives                                                                        | Actives                                |
| ---------- | --- | --- | ----- | ---------- | -------- | ----------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----------- | ---------- | ---------- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Fire       | 1/5 | 12  | 20    | Fire       | +0       | +0          | Fire       | Water                                    | 10d6 (35)   | 3d6 (10)   | 6d6 (21)   | 14/14/8/3/3/3                   |                                                                                 | Slam, Spit                             |
| Earth      | 1/5 | 12  | 20    | Earth      | +0       | +0          | Earth      | Air                                      | 10d6 (35)   | 3d6 (10)   | 6d6 (21)   | 14/14/8/3/3/3                   |                                                                                 | Slam                                   |
| Water      | 1/5 | 12  | 20    | Water      | +0       | +0          | Water      | Earth                                    | 10d6 (35)   | 3d6 (10)   | 6d6 (21)   | 14/14/8/3/3/3                   |                                                                                 | Slam, Spit                             |

## Weight, Size, and Mass Table
> Note that the Size mod applies to both the base size and the level growth.  Remember a "regular" level 1 slime is about 3 ft in diameter and weighs about 300 lbs and grows about 1 ft in diameter per level as well as 100 lbs per level. As the size of the slime goes up eventually it will start to take up more spaces and become a size large or bigger creature. For more information about sizing rules see [[1.6 Other Rules]]. For more information on the mass and it's uses see [[2.6 Materials]].
