## Golem Descriptions

**Fire Golem**
A dense, squat humanoid form carved from thick layers of solidified tar and burned pitch. The body is approximately five feet tall with a broad, barrel-shaped torso that measures about three feet wide at the shoulders. The outer layer is black, glossy, and sticky to the touch, like hardened tar that has been burned and reheated many times. The surface is covered in a network of wide, jagged cracks that run vertically down the chest and horizontally across the belly, and these cracks glow steadily with orange and red light from the flames burning just beneath the surface. Where the cracks intersect, flames occasionally flickers out like small fires catching wind. The material is softest and most pliable around the shoulders, elbows, hips, and knees where movement is needed, and these joints are visibly darker and more tar-like. The arms are thick and cylinder-shaped, ending in blunt, squared-off fists that are flattened from repeated impact. The legs are short and stumpy, dragging slightly as it walks due to the weight, with feet that are wide and flat. The head is a smooth, rounded dome about the size of a pumpkin, with no features at all, just a slightly darker patch on the front where heat radiates most intensely. The core sits deep in the chest cavity, a perfectly round sphere of dense, burning coal about the size of a cannonball, glowing white-hot at the center with orange cracks radiating outward. The heat the golem radiates is intense enough to cause sweating fifteen feet away, and the air above it shimmers constantly. The golem leaves melted tar footprints that cool into black, sticky marks whenever it stands still.

**Earth Golem**
A massive, hulking figure sculpted from tightly packed earth, clay, and stones of various sizes. The body stands approximately five feet tall but appears much larger due to its extreme bulk and density. Every part of the form is completely rigid with no visible joints or flexibility; instead, movement occurs through grinding shifts between different segments of compressed earth that rub against each other like tectonic plates. The surface is extremely rough and grainy, like coarse sandpaper, with embedded pebbles, fist-sized rocks, and occasional larger stones acting as natural armor. The torso is a thick cylinder with faint horizontal bands where different layers of soil compress together. The arms are thick columns about two feet in diameter, ending in massive fists that are just rounded masses of compacted earth. The legs are rooted to the ground as if grown from it, with feet that are wide and flat, pressing deep impressions into soft ground. The head is a squared block, slightly wider than the shoulders, with no facial features whatsoever, just a slightly recessed area on the front where the core sits embedded in solid stone. The core is a smooth, dark gray river stone about the size of a basketball, perfectly round and incredibly dense, set in the center of the chest and fused directly to the surrounding earth. Moss and lichen grow on the shoulders and back, suggesting centuries of perfect stillness.

**Water Golem**
A tall, undulating humanoid shape made entirely of contained liquid with no solid structure whatsoever. The body is approximately five feet tall, constantly shifting and changing shape, with the water never settling into one fixed form. The liquid is deep blue and semi-transparent, dense enough to obscure what is inside but clear enough to see roughly a foot through. The water flows constantly in slow currents within the body, circulating from the feet up through the torso and back down again in an endless cycle. Limbs are formed by pushing water outward into roughly arm-shaped extensions and pulling it back inward to retract them, with no bones or framework to define their shape. The surface has an unnaturally strong tension that holds the form together despite the liquid nature. Small bubbles rise constantly through the body from the bottom and pop at the surface, creating a faint gurgling sound. The core is a small, perfectly clear diamond crystal about the size of a fist, suspended dead-center in the chest and glowing faintly with blue light. The crystal spins slowly in one direction while the water around it flows in the opposite direction, creating a perpetual motion. The golem doesn't walk so much as roll and slosh across surfaces, leaving wet trails behind, and it makes a constant sound like water running over rocks.

**Air Golem**
A swirling humanoid figure made entirely from dense, swirling fog given solid form. The body is approximately five feet tall made from dark gray fog. The fog is dark gray with occasional lighter wisps mixed in, and small lightning bolts crackle randomly throughout the mass. The form is a dense humanoid shape held together by static charge. Arms are dense fog trailing off at the ends. The legs are denser fog at the bottom. The head is a slightly more solid area, a small dark stone about the size of a grapefruit. The core crackles with constant low-energy lightning that illuminates the fog. The golem moves by flowing quickly in one direction and reforming. The air around it feels heavy and charged, and the constant low rumbling can be heard from twenty feet away.

**Radiant Golem**
A towering, gleaming humanoid form sculpted entirely from solid gold. The body stands approximately five feet tall with perfectly proportioned limbs that catch and reflect light from every angle. The gold surface is polished to a mirror shine, with no imperfections or blemishes anywhere, reflecting the surroundings perfectly. The torso is a smooth cylinder with subtle curves where muscles would be, and the gold flows seamlessly from one part to the next with no visible seams or joints. The arms are perfectly round cylinders with hands that have five defined but blunt fingers each, every digit smooth and featureless. The legs are straight and sturdy, with feet that are slightly pointed at the toes. The head is a smooth, featureless oval about the size of a watermelon, with no nose, no mouth, no eyes, just perfectly polished gold. The core is embedded in the center of the chest, a massive flawless diamond about the size of a fist, glowing with steady golden light so intense that looking directly at it causes eye pain. The gold is heavy beyond its apparent size, pulling at the ground beneath it, and the golem moves with deliberate, measured steps that produce a soft chime with each footfall. The air around it carries the faint smell of incense, and plants bend toward it naturally as if following the sun.

**Necrotic Golem**
A lean, angular humanoid figure carved from solid black onyx. The body is approximately five feet tall but appears taller due to its sharp, angular build with no curves or soft edges anywhere. The stone is polished to a mirror shine but shows nothing in its reflections, only absolute black void, as if light is absorbed rather than reflected. The torso is narrow with visible ribs of darker stone jutting out from the sides, and sharp spines run down the back from neck to tailbone. The arms are thin and skeletal, ending in fingers that taper into wicked points like knifeblades. The legs are angular and unnatural, bending at wrong angles. The head is a perfect oval helmet shape with no features at all, just the faint glow of the core visible through cracks in the stone. Two shallow grooves run vertically down the face where features would be. The core is a small black sphere at the heart, no larger than a tennis ball, with sickly green light pulsing visible through micro-fractures in the onyx. Cold radiates from the golem in waves that can be felt from fifteen feet away, and frost patterns in the shape of hands appear on the surface during movement. The air around it smells of old tombs and decay.

**Psychic Golem**
A flowing, organic humanoid shape made from translucent amethyst crystal. The body is approximately five feet tall with limbs that curve and bend in ways that violate normal geometry - extending further than they should and bending at angles that don't work in normal space. The amethyst is cloudy and opaque, with purple light shifting through it constantly in wave-like patterns that resemble brain waves. The entire body contains a brain visible through crystal, with folds and ridges visible in the chest and head areas. The torso is roughly oval and slightly larger than natural proportions. Arms extend in long, sinuous curves with no distinct elbows or wrists, just smooth transitions from segment to segment. The head is a smooth dome with no features, but the amethyst is slightly clearer there, showing the core suspended inside. The core is a perfectly cut amethyst crystal about the size of a fist, floating in the center of the chest and rotating slowly while projecting purple light in pulsing waves that can be felt on the skin. The golem hovers slightly above the ground rather than walking, gliding silently. Those nearby report sudden headaches and intrusive thoughts, and the golem speaks in voices that seem to originate from inside the listener's own head.

**Lightning Golem**
A jagged, angular figure built from copper framework and quartz crystals. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall with a skeletal structure of copper bars and plates, and large quartz crystals jutting from joints and along the spine. Arcs of lightning crackle constantly between the crystals, surrounded by a perpetual lightning storm. The copper is dark and oxidized green in places, with bright copper showing where plates connect. Large quartz crystals protrude from the shoulders, spine, and back, ranging from the size of a fist to the size of a head, all crackling with electricity. The arms are copper cylinders with visible wiring running along them, ending in copper hands with five splayed fingers. The legs are thick copper cylinders with visible joints. The head is a copper helm that is slightly pointed at the top, with a constant glow of lightning visible from within. The core is a massive quartz cluster in the chest, about the size of two fists, glowing bright white from electricity. The golem moves in sudden bursts, disappearing from one spot and reappearing in another with a crack of thunder. Ozone precedes it, and hair stands on end twenty feet away.

**Steam Golem**
A bulky, rounded humanoid figure made from corroded metal and contained superheated water. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall with a barrel-shaped torso like a pressure vessel. The metal is heavily corroded and weathered, orange and brown rust mixed with dark iron, covered in mineral deposits that look like stalactites growing from old pipes. Steam constantly vents from breaks in the form, creating a perpetual fog around the golem that extends five feet in every direction. Multiple venting ports are visible along the shoulders, back, and joints, all streaming white-hot steam. The arms are thick pipes connected to the main body, ending in flattened copper fists. The legs are thick cylinder supports with visible rust and corrosion. The head is a domed helm with two small viewing slits that constantly stream steam, obscuring any view inside. The core is a small blue sapphire in the center of the chest, about the size of a fist, glowing dull red from the heat. The golem moves with a hissing, groaning shuffle that shakes loose rust and scale with every step. The air around it shimmers with heat distortion, and condensation drips constantly from its form, leaving wet, rust-stained spots on the ground.

**Magma Golem**
A towering, slow-moving figure made from black obsidian with rivers of molten magma flowing through it. The body stands approximately six feet tall with a bulky, irregular shape. The obsidian is black volcanic glass with a glossy, slightly rippled surface, broken by rivers and channels of glowing orange magma that flow from the chest outward to the limbs and back in a slow cycle. Chunks of solid obsidian break off and re-form as it moves, the glass constantly fracturing and healing. The torso is a massive block of black glass with magma flowing through visible channels carved into the surface. The arms are thick columns ending in massive, blocky fists that drag on the ground. The legs are like columns of partially cooled lava, wider at the top and narrower at the ankles. The head is a roughly angular block with two rivers of molten rock on the face, leaving trails of fire wherever it looks. The core is a sphere of white-hot magma at the heart, about the size of a cannonball, glowing so brightly it is nearly blinding. Heat radiates in intense waves, and the smell of sulfur fills the air. Smoke rises from every crack and joint. With each step, the ground cracks beneath it, and new fissures open.

**Ice/Cold Golem**
A towering, crystalline humanoid form carved from solid ice and enchanted frost. The body stands approximately five feet five inches tall with perfectly proportioned limbs that are crystal clear in some places and milky white in others. Frost patterns are trapped within the ice, visible as white streaks in the clear sections. The surface is polished to a mirror shine that reflects the surroundings perfectly, while frost and snow cling to the joints and accumulate on the shoulders. The torso is a perfectly clear cylinder with the core visible as a bright spot in the center. The arms are smooth columns that taper slightly at the wrists, ending in hands with visible five-digit impressions that are slightly模糊. The legs are straight columns with frost built up around the ankles. The head is an angular helm with a frozen glow visible from within, and ice crystallizes around the entire head in fractal patterns. The core is a perfectly clear diamond at the center, about the size of a fist, frozen so cold it bends light rather than reflecting it. The golem moves with precise, angular movements that creak and pop. Frost forms on everything within ten feet, and the cold can be felt from fifty feet away.

**Dust Golem**
A thin, barely-solid humanoid figure made from compacted dirt and dust particles. The body is approximately five feet tall but appears wispy and insubstantial, more cloud than form. The entire body is made of brown dust particles held together by some unknown force, constantly shedding small amounts and reforming. Parts of it collapse into piles on the ground before pulling back together, with debris constantly falling from the edges. The torso is a slightly more solid area where the core sits embedded. Arms are wispy trails of dust that sometimes form vague finger-shapes before falling apart. The legs are just denser dust at the bottom that trails behind. The head is a slightly more solid area with no features. The core is a tightly compacted sphere of superdense dust at its center, about the size of a grapefruit, darker and denser than the rest of the body. The golem moves by flowing across surfaces, leaving thin trails of dust in its wake. It can squeeze through impossibly small spaces and reform on the other side. The air around it is thick with floating particles, and coughing seizes anyone who breathes it in.

**Mud Golem**
A hulking, shapeless humanoid mass made from wet clay and compacted earth. The body is approximately six feet tall with no fixed shape, constantly shifting and blundering. The surface is dripping wet mud that stays wet, with chunks of denser material rising and sinking within the form like rafts on a river. The torso is a massive, uneven blob with no distinguishable shape. Arms are thick streams of mud that extend and retract unpredictably. The legs are just denser mud at the bottom that drags across the ground, leaving deep furrows. The head is an ill-defined mass with no features. The core is a smooth river stone at the heart, about the size of a bowling ball, coated in so much layered mud it appears black. The golem moves with a slow, plodding shamble that drags mud everywhere, picking up debris and growing larger as it moves. The smell is overwhelming, and its touch leaves stains.

**Nova Golem**
A brilliant, pulsing humanoid figure made from compressed solid hydrogen. The body is approximately six feet five inches tall with a perfectly proportioned humanoid shape that expands and contracts in steady rhythms. Every expansion sends waves of blinding white-gold light across the area, and every contraction causes the air to ring with barely-contained power. The hydrogen is so compressed it glows with its own light, with a golden-white color similar to the sun seen from orbit. The torso is a smooth cylinder with a bright gemstone core visible in the center. The arms are perfectly round cylinders with five blunt fingers each. The legs are straight and sturdy. The head is a perfect sphere that glows brighter than the rest, making the entire golem appear headless. The core is a bright yellow diamond at the heart, about the size of a cannonball, glowing so bright it pulses visibly. The golem moves in rapid pulses of light, appearing here and gone in flashes. The brightness can be seen for miles, and the force of its pulses can knock creatures prone.

**Solar Golem**
A blazing, golden-yellow humanoid figure made from bright golden crystal. The body stands approximately six feet tall and radiates with heat and light - glowing bright yellow-gold like the sun. The crystal is bright golden-yellow with an inner glow, like molten gold given solid form. The surface shimmers with heat distortion, and gold metallic veins are visible running through the form like capillaries. The torso is a perfectly round cylinder with the core visible as a blinding white spot in the center. The arms are smooth cylinders ending in five-blunt fingers. The legs are straight and slightly glowing. The head is a smooth golden helm that glows brighter than the rest, so bright it hurts to look at directly. The core is a bright golden-yellow diamond at the heart, about the size of a grapefruit, glowing so bright it is nearly impossible to see directly. The golem hovers slightly above ground, trailing superheated air. The heat is intense enough to make the area around it unbearably warm, and metals soften in its presence.

**Hallowed Golem**
A tall, pure humanoid form made from enchanted holy water given solid form. The body stands approximately five feet five inches tall with graceful, flowing limbs that defy gravity. The water is perfectly clear with golden light drifting through it in slow currents. The surface has an unnatural tension that holds the form despite being liquid. The torso is a smooth oval with the core visible as a glowing golden drop in the center. The arms are flowing shapes with vaguely hand-like ends. The legs are smooth columns that never quite touch the ground. The head is a smooth, watery helm with no features. The core is a small golden orb at its heart, about the size of a grape, glowing with steady sacred light. The golem moves with a flowing, graceful glide. It leaves perfectly clean footprints that smell of sacred springs. Its touch cleanses wounds and restores vitality.

**Starlight Golem**
A blazing, fiery humanoid figure made from iron framework wrapped in star-fire. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall with a skeletal iron structure engulfed in white-hot flame - the fire burns so bright it outshines the sun. The iron framework is blackened and warped from the extreme heat, barely visible beneath the flames. The fire is bright yellow-white at the core and orange at the edges, constantly burning with the intensity of a star. The torso is an iron ribcage engulfed in flame. The arms are iron pipes wrapped in fire. The legs are thick iron cylinders wrapped in flame. The head is an iron helm with flames pouring from every opening. The core is a bright white diamond at the center, about the size of a grapefruit, glowing with star-fire. The golem moves with a blazing float, trailing superheated air. The flames illuminate the area for hundreds of feet, and the heat is intense enough to melt metal nearby.

**Cursed Golem**
A jagged, uneven humanoid figure made from light green powder. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall made entirely of compacted light green powder. The powder is light green in color, like powdered malachite. The surface is rough and grainy, covered in powder that constantly sheds small amounts. The torso is a roughly humanoid shape of compressed powder. Arms are thick columns of powder ending in fists. Legs are thick columns, more stable at the bottom. The head is a jagged mass of powder with no features. The core is black obsidian at the heart, about the size of a grenade. It whispers constantly, and those nearby hear voices that aren't there.

**Ash Golem**
A thin, fragile humanoid figure made from compressed charcoal and ash. The body is approximately five feet tall - thin and barely holding form. The surface is grainy, rough charcoal with a matte black finish, flaking away at the edges constantly. The charcoal is cold to the touch but faintly warm near the center. The torso is a thin cylinder of compressed ash with visible flaking. Arms are thin and wiry, barely holding form. Legs are slightly thicker for stability. The head is a smoother mass at the top. The core is a red jasper at the center, about the size of a grapefruit, faintly warm and glowing. The golem moves with a slow, crumbling shamble that leaves ash trails everywhere.

**Blight Golem**
A twisted, rotting humanoid made from living decay and corrupted growth. The body is approximately six feet tall made from rotting materials. The surface is slick with decay, with pieces falling off. The material is a mix of rotting wood, diseased flesh, and fungal growth in various states of decomposition. The torso is a mass of overlapping growths. Arms are thick branches of rotting wood with fungal growths. Legs are rooted to the ground with thick roots. The head is a mess of vines and fungal growths with no features. The core is black obsidian at the heart, about the size of a fist. Everything it touches begins to rot, and new growth springs from its footprints.

**Poison Golem**
A slick, venomous humanoid figure made from concentrated poison given solid form. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall with a smooth, glass-like surface. The poison is green-black and thick, glowing faintly. Bubbles of gas rise constantly through the body and pop at the surface. The torso is a smooth oval with the core visible as a darker spot in the center. The arms are thick tubes of poison that drip constantly. The legs are more stable columns of contained poison. The head is a smooth dome with no features. The core is a green jade at its heart, about the size of a fist, so toxic that even looking at it makes eyes water. Everything it touches is contaminated.

**Toxin Golem**
A wispy, barely-solid humanoid figure made from breathable toxins given solid form. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall but constantly dissipates and reforms - at times more solid, then falling apart into yellow-green gas. The toxin is a thick, viscous gas that is heavier than air, pooling in low areas and rising slowly. The torso is the densest area, roughly oval in shape but constantly shifting. Arms are trailing wisps of gas that sometimes form vague finger-shapes before dissipating. Legs are just denser gas at the bottom that trails behind. The head is a slightly more solid area where the core sits embedded, about the size of a grapefruit, still essentially gas but more concentrated. The core is a dense cluster of toxins at the center, about the size of a basketball, glowing faintly yellow-green. The golem moves by flowing outward and reforming in a new place, leaving trails of toxic gas everywhere it passes. The smell is sweet and sickly simultaneously, and anyone breathing within thirty feet must make a constitution save or become poisoned. Small plants and grass die and wither in its wake, turning yellow then black.

**Bloodfire Golem**
A raging, burning humanoid figure made from bloodstone and hellfire. The body stands approximately six feet tall with a thick, muscular build - pulsing with contained fury. The stone is dark red, almost black, with thick veins of black fire running through it like blood vessels, and the fire beats like a heart in rhythm with the stone. The surface is rough and heat-scarred, with the hellfire showing through cracks in the bloodstone. The core is a living bloodfire heart at the center of the chest, about the size of a fist, glowing white-hot and beating like a real heart with a steady pulse. The head is a brutal helm shape with cracks showing internal fire. Arms are thick stone columns ending in massive fists. The legs are thick stone columns that drag slightly. When it attacks, it screams in a voice like burning screams, and the fire never goes out even underwater. Everything it touches catches bloodfire that burns through resistances.

**Tremor Golem**
A short, stocky humanoid figure made from vibrating earth and stone. The body is approximately five feet tall made from rough, volcanic rock with visible vibration patterns running through it in waves. The entire body hums with subsonic vibration that can be felt in the teeth. The core is a shaking stone at the heart, about the size of a bowling ball, vibrating constantly. The head is a squared block. Arms are thick columns with visible tremor lines. Legs are short and thick, rooted to the ground. Every step causes small earthquakes, and hard stamping can cause actual fissures in the earth.

**Deluge Golem**
A massive, crashing humanoid figure made from solid water. The body is approximately six feet tall shaped like rushing floodwater frozen mid-crash - arms reaching forward, one leg bent mid-stride and one leg pushed behind. The water is so compressed it holds shape, appearing liquid but solid. The torso is a curved shape like a wave cresting. The arms are extended forward like reaching waves. The legs are merged into the base of the wave. The core is superdense water at the heart, about the size of a basketball, under so much pressure it is nearly solid. Everything in its path is swept away and carried with it, the water adding to its mass as it moves.

**Shatter Golem**
A jagged, broken humanoid figure made from sharp glass. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall with all edges sharp and all surfaces broken - pieces of shattered mirrors held together. The glass is razor-sharp, with hundreds of cutting surfaces catching light at different angles. Pieces are held together by nothing visible, floating in relation to each other. The core is a sphere in the center, about the size of a grapefruit, glowing faintly. The head is a crown of broken glass that constantly reforms. The arms are jagged arrays of sharp shards. The legs are thicker columns of broken glass. Everything breaks on contact with it, and it breaks when struck, reassembling slowly.

**Sorrow Golem**
A bent, broken humanoid figure made from grief stone. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall - drooping forward with visible weight. The stone is heavy gray with visible tears cutting channels through its form like canyons. Every line droops with weight. The torso is a rounded mass that droops forward. Arms hang down with visible weight. Legs are thick columns but bent under weight. The head is a downturned mass with no features. The core is a heavy gray stone at the heart, about the size of a fist. Everything it touches feels heavier, and the air around it feels heavy with loss.

**Chaos Golem**
A shifting, unnatural humanoid figure made from chaos mushrooms and dirt. The body is approximately six feet five inches tall - the left side is solid mushroom while the right side is gas, then they swap. The dirt part is stable brown and solid, while the mushroom part shifts between forms - sometimes a mushroom cap, sometimes gas, sometimes liquid sludge. The head is a shifting mass. The arms are inconsistent - one solid mushroom arm reaching out, one gas arm that dissipates before contact. The legs are thick brown dirt columns that shift. The core is dark stone at the center, about the size of a basketball, swirling with mutating energy. Luck goes wrong near it - dice roll wrong, weapons break, spells fizzle. It is unstable chaos given form.

**Infernal Golem**
A burning, raging demon figure made from infernal iron and hellfire. The body is approximately six feet tall with a thick, demonic shape. The infernal iron is dark gray to black with visible hellfire cracking through the surface in glowing veins. The iron is hot to the touch, nearly melting, and the hellfire shows through every crack and seam. The surface is rough and heat-scarred, with the black fire veins pulsing with contained fury. The torso is a massive iron chest with hellfire visible through cracks. The arms are thick iron columns ending in brutal claw-like fists that drag on the ground. The legs are thick iron pillars that support the massive weight. The head is a horned helm with no features - just the glow of internal fire visible through cracks in the metal. The core is a sphere of hellfire at the heart, about the size of a basketball, burning with eternal fire that cannot be extinguished by any natural means. The fire bypasses all resistances and immunities - anything it touches catches infernal fire that spreads and burns forever. When it attacks, it screams in a voice like burning souls, and the sound carries for miles.

**Metal Golem**
A towering, unstoppable humanoid figure made from tungsten, lead, and titanium. The body is approximately six feet five inches tall with perfect machine proportions, every joint perfectly fitted, every surface precision-machined. The tungsten is dark gray with a metallic shine, gleaming in light. The lead is heavier and duller, making up the bulk of the body. The titanium is lighter but stronger, reinforcing joints and load-bearing areas. Every joint is a visible connection - shoulder joints, elbow joints, hip joints, knee joints - all perfectly machined to fit together. The torso is a massive cylinder of fitted plates, lead between tungsten layers for weight and titanium for strength. The arms are thick cylinders with visible joints at the shoulder and elbow, ending in massive fists that weigh more than most creatures carry. The legs are thick pillars with visible knee joints, each foot a wide, flat platform that crushes stone. The head is a smooth helm with no features - not even eye slots, just polished metal. The core is a sphere of pure dense metal at the heart, about the size of a basketball, heavier than it should be. It weighs far more than it appears - its mass bends the ground beneath it, and it crushes through anything in its path. Every step leaves deep impressions in stone, and walls crumble when it pushes through them. Nothing stops it once it starts moving.

**Torrent Golem**
A flowing, directional humanoid figure made from high-velocity solid water. The body is approximately six feet tall shaped like a wave frozen mid-crash - arms reach forward in a reaching pose, one leg bent mid-stride and one leg pushed off behind. The solid water is so compressed it is nearly solid, holding its forward-crashing shape like ice but still appearing liquid. The water visible within flows in one direction only, from the back foot pushing forward through the body to the reaching hands. The torso is a curved shape like a wave cresting forward, with the solid water compressed so tightly it holds like ice but looks liquid. The arms are extended forward in a reaching pose, with the water flowing through them. The legs are thick columns with one bent at the knee mid-step and one pushed off forcefully behind. The core is superdense water at the heart, about the size of a basketball, under so much pressure it is nearly solid but still flowing internally. Everything in its path is swept away and carried with it - creatures smashed against walls, swept along in the current, added to its mass as it moves. The sound of its movement is like a flash flood - roaring and crashing and thundering. It never stops moving once it starts, unstoppable as the water itself.

**Thunder Golem**
A rumbling, echoing humanoid figure made from vibration-storing stone. The body is approximately six feet tall with a dense, chunky build - constantly humming and vibrating. The stone is dark gray and rough, with visible vibration patterns running through it in concentric waves that can be seen rippling across the surface. The air around it shimmers with sonic tension, and sound builds and builds with every movement. The torso is a thick cylinder with the core visible as a brighter spot in the center where vibration is strongest. The arms are thick columns that end in blunt stone fists. The legs are thick, sturdy columns with visible cracks forming around the vibration points. The head is a rounded helm with no features, just vibration lines around it. The core is a stone at the heart, about the size of a basketball, full of stored sound vibrating so intensely it makes the stone hum audibly. Every movement stores more vibration, and it can release devastating sonic attacks on command that shatter stone and knock creatures prone. The constant rumbling can be heard from fifty feet away, and the vibration can be felt in the bones.

**Mirage Golem**
A wavering humanoid figure made from solid glass. The body is approximately five feet five inches tall - the glass is semi-transparent and refractive. The surface is iridescent. The torso is a roughly oval shape. The arms are glass columns. The legs are thick glass columns. The head is a smooth dome with no features. The core is clear quartz in the center, about the size of a grapefruit. The golem is difficult to target in combat. Attacks pass through, then the golem appears somewhere else.

**Vacuum Golem**
A pulling, void-shaped figure made from vacuum mass. The body is approximately six feet five inches tall appearing as a perfect silhouette of nothing - a hole in the shape of a person cut from reality. The vacuum mass is so perfectly dark that it shows nothing - no reflection, no light, no shadow, just absence given form. The air around it thins visibly, and nearby objects stretch slightly toward it. The torso is a perfect humanoid void shape. The arms are void columns that pull everything toward them. The legs are void pillars rooted in place by their pull. The head is a smooth oval of absolute blackness, no features, no light, nothing. The core is black obsidian at the heart, about the size of a pineapple, containing the void. Everything nearby - air, light, sound, thoughts - is pulled toward it. The air thins until creatures nearby gasp for breath.

**Warp Golem**
A spiraling, twisting humanoid figure made from warpstone. The body is approximately six feet five inches tall and bends through dimensions, appearing in places it shouldn't be able to reach. The warpstone is purple-blue with visible folds in the stone - like layers of reality stacked on top of each other but not quite aligned, making the stone hard to look at. The surface has a dizzying quality to look at - the eye slides off, unable to focus on it. The torso is a twisted column with folds running through it - the body bends where it shouldn't bend. The arms bend at angles that don't work in normal space - one moment behind the back, the next in front, appearing without crossing the space between. The legs are thick columns with the same folding quality - walking in spirals. The head is a spiraling mass with no real features - just a twisted shape. The core is purple quartz at the heart, about the size of a basketball, swirling with dimensional energy. It can appear behind enemies without crossing the space between - space folds around it like paper being crumpled.

**Storm Golem**
A raging, electrical figure made from solid cloud and copper. The body is approximately seven feet tall with a towering, storm-like appearance - massive and looming with crackling energy. The cloud-flesh is dark gray and dense, like a thunderhead given solid form. Copper veins run through it like lightning rods - visible pathways that carry electrical charge. Small lightning crackles throughout the cloud randomly, illuminating the form in brief flashes that come and go. The air around it tastes of ozone and feels charged. The core is a dense copper-quartz cluster at the heart, about the size of a basketball, crackling with unimplemented power. The torso is a massive cloud shape with copper veins pulsing through it - visible lightning paths. The arms are cloud columns ending in cloud fists - heavy and dense. The legs are thick cloud columns that trail off into mist at the bottom - not quite touching the ground. The head is a cloud mass with no features - just a darker area where the storm is densest. Every movement produces thunder that shakes windows and rattles bones, and the air tastes of ozone. Constant small lightning keeps the area illuminated in strobe light.

**Frostfire Golem**
A screaming, splitting humanoid figure made from dry ice. The body is approximately seven feet tall and is split exactly down the middle - one half burning with fire, the other freezing with cold, both at once. The dry ice is white-blue on the frozen side with frost patterns and red-orange on the burning side with ember patterns, with a clear split line down the center where steam constantly rises. The temperature shifts are extreme - standing on the fire side burns, on the ice side freezes. The core is at the heart, about the size of a basketball, somehow containing both states at once. The torso is split exactly in half, fire on one side, ice on the other - the boundary visible as a constant line of steam. The arms are similarly split - one fire arm burning with visible flames and one ice arm frozen solid with frost. The legs are split the same way - one leg on each side of the temperature boundary. The head is split down the middle - half fire, half ice. Thermal shock damages anything it touches - the fire burns and the cold freezes at the same time, shattering most materials instantly. The air screams with temperature shifts, going from burning hot to freezing cold in alternating seconds.

**Glacial Golem**
A towering, crushing figure made from ancient ice and frozen water. The body is approximately seven feet six inches tall with a massive, immovable build - wide as two people and heavy as a glacier. The ice is blue-white and ancient, with frozen currents visible within that have been frozen since before recorded history - entire rivers frozen in time. Frost patterns cover every surface, intricate and detailed, and snow accumulates on the shoulders and head constantly, never melting. The core is frozen water at the heart, about the size of a basketball, frozen so cold it has become ice again - perpetual winter given form. The torso is a massive ice column with visible frozen currents running through it like rivers frozen in time - entire ecosystems preserved in the ice. The arms are thick ice columns ending in ice fists - heavy enough to crush stone. The legs are thick ice columns rooted to the ground, sunk deep into frozen earth. The head is a crown of ice with snow piled on top - a frozen crown for a frozen king. Everything it touches freezes instantly, and frost forms permanently in its path - grass, stone, metal all frost over. The cold can be felt from fifty feet away, and the air freezes in breath clouds even in summer.

**Void Golem**
A small, perfectly dark figure made from voidsand. The body is approximately seven feet six inches tall but appears as a hole in the world - a perfect humanoid silhouette cut from nothing, so perfectly dark it absorbs all light rather than reflecting it. The voidsand is black so complete that looking at it is like looking at nothing at all - a shaped absence given form. The surface is smooth and flat like polished stone but darker than dark. The air around it thins visibly, and nearby objects stretch slightly toward it. The torso is a perfect humanoid void shape - no details, just the suggestion of a body. The arms are void columns that pull everything toward them. The legs are void pillars rooted in place by their pull. The head is a smooth oval of perfect blackness with no features - no eyes, no nose, no mouth, nothing. The core is black opal at the heart, about the size of a grenade, containing the perfect darkness. Everything light nearby is pulled toward it - photons, reflections, shadows all stretch and flow toward the void. The darkness around it is absolute - no light escapes, no reflection exists, no shadow can form. It is the empty made solid.

**Obsidian Golem**
A terrifying, sharp figure made from volcanic glass. The body is approximately seven feet six inches tall with gleaming black surfaces that catch light in hundreds of places - sharp reflections from every angle. The obsidian is volcanic glass, jet black with a glossy surface that reflects perfectly like a mirror. Every surface is razor-sharp, every edge honed by volcanic heat to cut through anything. The glass is smooth and gleaming but dangerous to touch. The torso is a massive block of black glass with visible magma veins running through it - dull red glowing cracks that pulse with heat. The arms are thick columns ending in blade-like fists with sharp edges. The legs are thick columns with blade edges on the inside of the legs. The head is a polished helm with no features - smooth and gleaming black glass. The core is magma at the heart, about the size of a basketball, glowing dull red through the obsidian. Everything that strikes it shatters against the glass - weapons break, armor cracks, bones splinter. It cuts through almost anything it touches.

**Quake Golem**
A short, heavy figure made from sandstone earthquake. The body is approximately seven feet six inches tall but appears short and squat due to its massive width and density - wide as a doorway and heavy as a building. The sandstone is brown-gray with visible earthquake patterns running through it in fault lines - cracks that show the earth's rage contained within. Every step causes tremors that spread outward in waves. The torso is a massive cylinder with fault lines visible throughout - cracks showing ancient earthquakes frozen in the stone. The arms are thick columns ending in massive fists that weigh tons. The legs are pillars that drive into the ground with every step, leaving deep footprints. The head is a boulder mass with no features - just a rough rounded shape. The core is earthquake at the heart, about the size of a basketball, containing the earth's contained rage. Every footfall is an earthquake - buildings collapse, walls crack, ground splits. Cracks spread from where it steps, splitting the ground open into fissures.

**Corruption Golem**
A mutating, terrible figure made from corrupt resin. The body is approximately eight feet tall with a constantly shifting, changing form that never stays the same - one moment round, the next elongated, the next lumpy. The corrupt resin is black-green and slick, with different stages of corruption visible throughout - some places rotting and falling apart, some places spreading new growth, some places mutating into new forms. Things grow and die on it constantly - fungal growths pop up and wither, rot spreads and dries, new corruption bursts through. The surface is slick with decaying matter, dripping constantly. The torso is a blob of overlapping corruption with things pushing through - bones, teeth, and tendrils all emerging and receding. The arms are thick branches of corruption that reach out with tendrils dripping, some ending in rotting fists. The legs are rooted to the ground with thick roots spreading corruption into the earth below. The head is a shifting mass with no features - just a darker patch that might be a face. The core is black jade at the heart, about the size of a basketball, pulsing with rot. Everything it touches becomes corrupt, spreading sickness and rot that spreads to everything it touches.

**Miasma Golem**
A sickening, cloud-like figure made from plague sludge. The body is approximately seven feet six inches tall and appears as a shifting, dripping mass of yellow-green sludge given humanoid form - thick at the top where the head is and spreading downward into a puddle base. The plague sludge is thick and sickening, dripping constantly with long ropes of sludge falling and pooling at its base. The surface is lumpy and uneven, with different consistencies - some spots thick and chunky, some spots thin and runny. The smell is overwhelming - like rotting corpses and diseased flesh combined. The torso is a massive blob of sludge with visible chunks floating within - pieces of rot, bone, and flesh all suspended. The arms are thick drips that hang down and occasionally reach out. The legs are just the sludge base that spreads across the ground. The head is a cloud mass with no features - just a vaguely head-shaped area of thicker sludge. The core is black jade at the heart, about the size of a basketball, thick and pulsing with disease. Everything nearby becomes sick just from proximity - creatures within twenty feet must make constitution saves or become poisoned and sickened. The air around it becomes toxic, and creatures breathing the air near it cough and choke. Plants and grass die and wither in its path, turning black and rotting.

**Gel Golem**
A massive, blob-like humanoid figure made from acidic gel. The body is approximately nine feet tall and nine feet wide with no real shape, just a massive amorphous blob of clear acidic gel that shifts and ripples constantly. The gel is completely clear and transparent with dissolved armor, weapons, and creatures all visible floating within - bits of steel, bones, cloth, and flesh all dissolving together into sludge. The surface is constantly rippling and shifting, with gel flowing from one area to another like a slow wave. The torso is just the main blob mass with no defined shape. The arms are thick drips that extend and retract unpredictably, sometimes forming vague hand-shapes before dissolving back into the blob. The legs are just the base of the blob, dragging across the ground leaving thick gel trails that take forever to clean up. The head is a slightly more defined area at the top with no features - just a smoother dome of gel where the core floats suspended. The core is clear quartz at the center, about the size of a basketball, pulsing with dissolving energy. Everything that touches it begins dissolving immediately - flesh, armor, weapons, stone all dissolve into acidic sludge. After dissolving approximately one hundred pounds of material, the gel can no longer dissolve anything. Glass, nerite, voidsteel, and vacuum mass are immune to the dissolving. The gel can reform itself after being cut apart - pieces flow back together over several turns. The acidic smell rises constantly, like rotting things and burning chemicals combined.

**Atomic Golem**
A glowing, radioactive figure made from uranium. The body is approximately eight feet six inches tall with a rough, crystalline surface. The uranium glows with sickly green-white radioactivity, illuminating the area in an unhealthy light. The surface is rough and crystalline, with visible cracks running through it from the constant radiation damage. The air around it shimmers with radiation. The torso is a glowing mass of uranium with cracks running through it. The arms are rough columns ending in fists. The legs are thick pillars. The head is a rough helmet with no features. The core is green zircon at the heart, about the size of a basketball, glowing with radiation. Everything nearby becomes radioactive, and creatures within thirty feet must make constitution saves or gain radiation condition.

**Eldritch Golem**
A thick, goupy purple-black figure made from unstable matter. The body is approximately nine feet tall with a shapeless, blob-like form that constantly drips and reforms, like cold tar given form. The material is thick and sticky like cold tar, purple-black with darker purple chunks floating within. The surface is uneven and lumpy, with chunks constantly breaking off and reattaching. The torso is a massive oval blob with no real shape, just a vaguely humanoid mass. The arms are thick drips that extend and retract unpredictably, sometimes five feet long, sometimes barely there. The legs are just the base of the blob, dragging across the ground leaving thick purple-black trails. The head is a slightly more defined area at the top with no features - no eyes, no mouth, no nose, just a darker patch where the core floats. The core is a dense sphere of unstable matter at the center, about the size of a basketball, purple-black and pulsing with sickly purple light that hurts to look at. The golem moves by flowing slowly across surfaces like thick syrup, leaving sticky purple-black trails everywhere it passes. Everything it touches dissolves into purple-black sludge that smells like rotting magic - not decay, not fire, but wrongness. The material is heavy and clings to everything it touches, and it takes forever to clean off.




========================================================================
  MARKDOWN: Eyum TTRPG/7.0 Monsters (WIP)/7.3 Monster Lists/7.3.3 Dragons.md
========================================================================

Dragons are large, winged reptilian creatures composed of dense biological tissue suffused with elemental energy throughout every cell. Unlike slimes or golems, dragons are fully organic organisms with complete skeletal, muscular, circulatory, and nervous systems, all of which are infused with their elemental affinity. Their flesh, blood, and scales all carry traces of their element, but the core difference between a dragon and a mundane creature is the concentration of this energy. A dragon does not channel its element so much as it is physically made of it: fire dragons have blood that burns, ice dragons have lungs that freeze the air inside them, and lightning dragons have nervous systems that fire in constant, visible arcs.

A dragon's body follows a standard tetrapod layout with two forelimbs, two hind limbs, and two wings. The wings are supported by a modified skeletal structure where elongated finger bones stretch a membrane of thick, element-infused hide. The forelimbs and hind limbs are heavily muscled and end in claws that are dense, pointed growths of hardened keratin and crystallized elemental residue. The tail is long and heavy, used for balance during flight and as a blunt weapon on the ground. The head is large relative to the body, housing a developed brain, sensory organs, and the specialized organ clusters required for elemental breath attacks. The jaw structure allows for a wide gape and powerful bite force, with teeth that are broad, conical, and reinforced with the same crystalline deposits found in the claws.

At the center of a dragon's circulatory system sits the *dragon heart*, a large, multi-chambered organ that serves as both a biological pump and an elemental condenser. The heart draws ambient elemental energy from the dragon's surroundings and from its own metabolic processes, concentrating it into the bloodstream. This element-saturated blood circulates through the body, feeding each tissue with the energy needed to maintain the dragon's passive elemental effects and active abilities. The heart is the only truly vital organ—damage to the brain, lungs, or spine will impair the dragon but not kill it. Only the complete destruction of the heart results in death, and dragons have evolved dense layers of muscle, bone, and scale to protect this organ specifically.

Dragons grow throughout their lives, adding mass and elemental capacity as they age. Growth is driven by the heart's ability to condense elemental energy into new biological tissue. A dragon that feeds regularly and fights often will grow faster than one that does not, but even a sedentary dragon will continue accumulating elemental energy over time, slowly increasing in size and power. Each level represents a measurable increase in the dragon's physical dimensions and elemental output. A dragon will gain a level approximately every five years if it is sedentary but if it is eating lots and fighting it can gain a level up to every year. 

- A Level 1 dragon is approximately:
    - **Length:** 20 ft
    - **Wingspan:** 30 ft
    - **Weight:** 2,000 lbs
- Each level:
    - +5 ft length
    - +5 ft wingspan
    - +400 lbs weight

Dragons reproduce through sexual reproduction with distinct male and female individuals. Mating requires specific environmental conditions tied to the element of the dragons involved, fire dragons mate near active volcanoes, ice dragons meet in high-altitude frozen regions, storm dragons mate during severe thunderstorms. The specific trigger conditions are not fully understood, but it is known that dragons will only attempt reproduction when their territory is secure, their elemental energy reserves are full, and the environmental conditions are matched to their element. After mating, the female carries a clutch of fertilized eggs internally for several months. The number of eggs varies by type, typically ranging from 3 to 12. The eggs are laid in a carefully constructed nest infused with the parent's elemental energy, nests are built in locations that naturally provide the required elemental conditions, such as geothermal vents, deep earth fissures, or high-altitude cloud formations. The eggs are leathery and semi-translucent, with the developing embryo visible as a dark shape within a glowing elemental fluid. Incubation takes 8 to 14 months depending on the type. Hatchlings emerge fully formed and immediately capable of elemental breath attacks, though at a fraction of the power of an adult (stats approx 1/4 that of a level 1 dragon, after approx 10 years they will reach level 1). Dragons cannot mate until reaching level 5. 

When a dragon dies, its elemental saturation causes a rapid release of stored energy. The body releases this energy in a visible discharge that lasts roughly an hour, during which the softer tissues dissolve into elemental vapor. The denser structures, scales, bones, and concentrated muscle tissue, remain intact and can be harvested. Approximately 30% of the dragon's pre-death weight is recoverable as solid material, with the remaining 70% dissolving into elemental vapor and being lost.

All dragons understand Dragonic. They cannot speak/converse freely in it, its more of just intent and general understanding. Once a dragon reaches level 5 they can speak dragonic as well. 

The harvested materials break down into three categories:
- **Dragon Scales:** Each dragon type has unique scales with distinct properties. Scales make up approximately 5% of a dragon's body weight. These scales retain the dragon's elemental affinity and are used to craft powerful equipment.
- **Dragon Meat:** The muscle tissue of dragons is infused with elemental energy. Dragon meat is a universal material that can be used in various ways. It makes up approximately 15% of a dragon's body weight.
- **Dragon Bones:** The skeletal structure of dragons is incredibly dense and retains elemental properties. Dragon bones are a universal material used for crafting and alchemy. Bones make up approximately 10% of a dragon's body weight.

## Stat Block Table for All Dragons
> For more information on how to level up a monster see [[7.1 Monsters]].

Dragons can serve any role depending on their element and build.

**All dragons possess the following passive abilities:**
- **Elemental Aura (30 ft)**: Dragons emit an aura of their element in a 30 ft radius. Creatures within this aura at the start of their turn take damage equal to the dragon's Affinity + half their level. This damage does not trigger resistances or weaknesses. The dragon can choose to suppress this aura at any time.
- **Flight**: Dragons have a flying speed equal to their listed speed. They can hover.
- **Elemental Form**: Dragons are immune to damage from their own element. Additionally, Dragons can move through their own element without taking damage or being hindered. The immunity granted from Elemental Form cannot be removed or reduced in any way, even from other effects which would normally ignore immunity. Instead of having regular Weakness to their respective opposite elements all dragons have lesser weakness. 
- **Dragon Vitality**: A dragon cannot take health damage by any means until its Vitality is totally exhausted. Any effect which would be split or go straight to HP will instead all go into Vitality until there is no more Vitality left. Any effect which would rely on Health specifically (like life steel) will do nothing and instantly fail at long as any vitality remains. 

> For condition references see [[2.4 Conditions]].

**Unique Skills for Specific Dragons:**
- **Elemental Breath (Active)**: (4 Mana) 30 ft cone. Targets take 3d8 + Affinity damage of the dragon's element type. Targets who fail a DEX save (DC 15 + Affinity) take half damage.
- **Elemental Wing Buffet (Active)**: (3 Mana) All creatures in a 15 ft radius must make a DEX save (DC 15 + Affinity) or take 2d8 + Affinity damage of the dragon's element type and be pushed 10 ft away. Flying creatures have disadvantage on this save.
- **Solar Annihilation (Active)**: (6 Mana) 100 ft line. Targets take 10d10 + Affinity radiant damage. Undead and creatures vulnerable to radiant take an additional 5d10 damage.
- **Supernova (Active)**: (6 Mana) 100 ft radius. Targets must make a CON save (DC 20 + Affinity) or take 15d10 + Affinity radiant damage and be blinded for 3 turns. Those who succeed take half damage and aren't blinded. The dragon takes 10d10 damage from the exertion.
- **Molten Core (Active)**: (6 Mana) The dragon explodes in a 30 ft radius, dealing 8d10 + Affinity fire damage to all creatures. The dragon then reforms at full health after 3 rounds but is stunned during this time.
- **Absolute Zero (Active)**: (6 Mana) 60 ft radius. All creatures in range must make a CON save (DC 20 + Affinity) or take 6d10 + Affinity cold damage and gain 10 stacks of the Frozen condition. Those who succeed take half damage and 3 stacks.
- **Desert Domain (Active)**: (4 Mana) Transform the battlefield into a desert for 1 hour. While active, the dragon is hidden within the sand and can take the Hide action as a bonus action. Additionally, any creature that takes damage from the dragon loses 50% of its movement speed for 1 turn.
- **Swallowed by the Earth (Active)**: (6 Mana) 50 ft radius. The ground beneath all creatures becomes quicksand. Creatures must make a DEX save (DC 15 + Affinity) or be restrained and take 3d6 + Affinity bludgeoning damage at the start of each turn.
- **Solar Fusion (Active)**: (6 Mana) The dragon merges with the sun for 1 round. During this time, it is invulnerable and can take no actions, but all creatures in a 100 ft radius take 10d10 + Affinity radiant damage at the start of each of the dragon's turns. After 1 round, the dragon reappears.
- **Cosmic Form (Active)**: (4 Mana) The dragon transforms into pure starlight for 1 round. While in this form, it can move through any obstacle and cannot be targeted by attacks. At the end of its turn, it reforms and all creatures within 20 ft take 6d10 + Affinity radiant damage.
- **Curse of Ruin (Active)**: (6 Mana) 60 ft radius. All creatures must make a WIS save (DC 18 + Affinity) or have disadvantage on all rolls and all attacks against the dragon have advantage for 1 hour. Additionally, whenever an affected creature rolls a 1, they take 5d10 + Affinity curse damage.
- **Ash Zombie (Active)**: (3 Mana) When the dragon reduces a creature to 0 HP, that creature rises as an ash zombie under the dragon's control at the start of the dragon's next turn. Ash zombies have HP equal to the creature's Level x 5 and deal 1d6 + Level fire damage on each hit.
- **Plague of Wither (Active)**: (6 Mana) 60 ft radius. All creatures must make a CON save (DC 18 + Affinity) or gain 20 stacks of the Withered condition. Those who succeed gain 10 stacks.
- **Venom Empire (Active)**: (3 Mana) Spawn 1d4 giant poisonous snakes from its body each round as a bonus action. These snakes have HP equal to the dragon's Level x 5 and can make a bite attack dealing 2d8 + Level poison damage plus the poisoned condition.
- **Biochemical Warfare (Active)**: (6 Mana) 80 ft radius cloud of toxic gas. Creatures must make a CON save (DC 18 + Affinity) or take 8d10 + Affinity toxin damage and gain the Suffocating condition for 3 turns. Those who succeed take half damage.
- **Blood Ritual (Active)**: (4 Mana) Sacrifice 50% of current health to deal that same amount as damage to all creatures in a 40 ft radius. Creatures can make a DEX save for half damage. The dragon then regains health equal to the damage dealt.
- **Seismic Smash (Active)**: (6 Mana) 100 ft radius. All creatures must make a DEX save (DC 18 + Affinity) or take 10d10 + Affinity bludgeoning damage and be stunned for 2 turns. Those who succeed take half damage and aren't stunned.
- **Tsunami (Active)**: (6 Mana) 100 ft line, 40 ft wide. Creatures in the path must make a STR save (DC 18 + Affinity) or take 8d10 + Affinity bludgeoning damage and be carried 60 ft away. The wave also deals 4d10 + Affinity water damage on the following turn.
- **Crystal Shards (Active)**: (4 Mana) 60 ft radius. Each creature takes 4d10 + Affinity slashing damage and must make a DEX save (DC 16 + Affinity) or take an additional 2d10 + Affinity damage from embedded shards at the start of their next turn.
- **Despair Incarnate (Active)**: (6 Mana) 60 ft radius. All creatures must make a WIS save (DC 18 + Affinity) or gain the Despair condition and take 6d10 + Affinity psychic damage. Creatures that succeed are immune to this effect for 24 hours.
- **Reality Fracture (Active)**: (6 Mana) 50 ft radius. Each creature in the area must roll on the Wild Magic table. The dragon is immune to all effects and can choose the result of its own roll.
- **Hellfire Storm (Active)**: (6 Mana) 60 ft radius storm of infernal fire that lasts for 3 turns. Creatures that start their turn in the storm take 5d10 + Affinity infernal damage. The dragon can move this storm up to 30 ft each turn as a bonus action.
- **Iron Dominion (Active)**: (4 Mana) Summon 1d4 iron constructs that are under its control. These constructs have HP equal to the dragon's Level x 10 and AC 20. They act on the dragon's initiative and can make slam attacks dealing 3d8 + Level damage.
- **Flood (Active)**: (4 Mana) Transform into a massive wave for up to 3 turns. While in wave form, it has no attacks but can move through creatures and objects. Any creature the wave moves through takes 6d10 + Affinity water damage and must make a STR save (DC 16 + Affinity) or be carried 30 ft.
- **Sonic Devastation (Active)**: (6 Mana) 80 ft radius. All creatures must make a CON save (DC 18 + Affinity) or take 10d10 + Affinity thunder damage and be deafened for 1 minute. Those who succeed take half damage.
- **Illusory Domination (Active)**: (4 Mana) Create 3 perfect illusory copies of itself. These copies have HP equal to the dragon's Level x 5 and can make attacks that deal half the dragon's normal damage. The dragon can see through any of its copies' senses.
- **Event Horizon (Active)**: (6 Mana) 40 ft radius region of extreme gravity. Creatures in the area must make a STR save (DC 18 + Affinity) or be pulled to the center and restrained. Any creature that starts its turn in the area takes 4d10 + Affinity void damage.
- **Spatial Tear (Active)**: (6 Mana) 50 ft radius. All creatures in the area are pulled toward the center and take 8d10 + Affinity force damage. Additionally, the dragon can teleport to any location within 100 ft as a bonus action for 3 turns.
- **Tempest King (Active)**: (6 Mana) Control all weather within 1 mile. Can cause hurricanes, tornadoes, or thunderstorms at will. Additionally, summon 1d6 lightning elementals that obey its commands.
- **Thermal Cycle (Active)**: (4 Mana) Rapidly alternate between fire and ice attacks. Each attack that deals fire damage also deals 1d6 cold damage, and vice versa. Additionally, unleash a blast dealing 10d10 + Affinity fire and 10d10 + Affinity cold damage to all creatures in a 40 ft radius.
- **Ice Age (Active)**: (6 Mana) Plunge the entire battlefield into a glacial age for 10 turns. All water on the battlefield freezes, and all creatures take 2d10 + Affinity cold damage at the start of each turn. The dragon can move through ice at full speed.
- **Abyssal Hunger (Active)**: (6 Mana) 60 ft radius. All light is consumed. Creatures must make a CON save (DC 18 + Affinity) or take 6d10 + Affinity void damage and gain the Soul Shattered condition. Additionally, consume one corpse per turn to heal 3d10 + Affinity HP.
- **Volcanic Eruption (Active)**: (6 Mana) 100 ft radius. All creatures must make a DEX save (DC 20 + Affinity) or take 12d10 + Affinity fire damage and be buried under rubble (restrained). Those who succeed take half damage.
- **Earthbreaker (Active)**: (6 Mana) A fissure 10 ft wide and 100 ft long opens, dealing 10d10 + Affinity bludgeoning damage to all creatures in the area. Creatures that fall in take an additional 10d10 + Affinity damage and are trapped at the bottom.
- **Corruption Incarnate (Active)**: (4 Mana) Transform into a being of pure corruption for 3 turns. While transformed, all attacks deal an additional 3d10 + Affinity corruption damage, and any creature that hits the dragon with a melee attack gains the Corrupt condition.
- **Pandemic (Active)**: (6 Mana) 100 ft radius. All creatures must make a CON save (DC 20 + Affinity) or gain 15 stacks of the Plagued condition and 15 stacks of the Poisoned condition. Those who succeed gain 5 stacks of each.
- **Dissolution (Active)**: (6 Mana) Dissolve into a pool of gel and reform elsewhere. While dissolved, can move through any opening and cannot be targeted. At the end of the turn, reform and all creatures within 15 ft take 6d10 + Affinity acid damage.
- **Nuclear Fusion (Active)**: (6 Mana) 100 ft radius. All creatures must make a CON save (DC 22 + Affinity) or take 20d10 + Affinity radiant damage and gain the Radiation condition with 20 stacks. The dragon takes 10d10 damage from the exertion.
- **Reality Collapse (Active)**: (8 Mana) 200 ft radius. All creatures must make an INT save (DC 30 + Affinity) or take 30d10 + Affinity eldritch damage and be transported to a random plane. Those who succeed take 15d10 + Affinity damage. The dragon takes 20d10 damage from the exertion.

## Weight, Size, and Material Table
> Note that the Size mod applies to both the base size and the level growth. Remember a "regular" level 1 dragon is about 20 ft long and weighs about 2,000 lbs and grows about 5 ft longer per level as well as 400 lbs per level. As the size of the dragon goes up eventually it will start to take up more spaces and become a size huge or bigger creature. For more information about sizing rules see [[1.6 Other Rules]]. For more information on the materials and their uses see [[2.6 Materials]].

> When a dragon is killed, approximately 30% of its weight can be harvested. The breakdown is approximately 5% scales, 15% meat, and 10% bones. The remaining 70% dissolves into elemental vapor and is lost.

| Name       | Weight (lbs) | Size (ft) | Size       | Role      | Scales (5%)                     | Meat (15%)                      | Bones (10%)                     |

| Earth      | x2.0 (4000)  | x1 (20)   | Gargantuan | Tanker    | Earth Dragon Scale (200 lbs)    | Dragon Meat (600 lbs)           | Dragon Bones (400 lbs)          |
| Water      | x1.2 (2400)  | x1 (20)   | Gargantuan | Mixed     | Water Dragon Scale (120 lbs)    | Dragon Meat (360 lbs)           | Dragon Bones (240 lbs)          |
| Air        | x0.8 (1600)  | x1 (20)   | Gargantuan | Ranger    | Air Dragon Scale (80 lbs)       | Dragon Meat (240 lbs)           | Dragon Bones (160 lbs)          |
